odor.description: Faint, slightly fatty-fruity with mild ester note; nearly odorless; FDA direct food additive (E1518) used as fragrance carrier and humectant in non-alcoholic media; common plasticizer for cellulose acetate filters and tobacco

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no concentration restriction; FEMA 2007 GRAS; FDA 21 CFR 184.1901 GRAS; EU food additive E1518
safety.usage: Typical 5-50% as carrier in alcohol-free fragrance, lozenges, flavour systems; non-irritant; pleasant skin feel

odor.description: Cleaner, sweeter, fruity-woody with characteristic resinous-balsamic-honey depth and softer animalic undertone vs. Hindi; defining Aquilaria crassna chemotype — favoured for fine fragrance niche use; sweet-leathery vs. Hindi's barnyard-fecal; ultra-premium (~$15000-50000/kg)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Aquilaria crassna oil: no specific concentration restriction; CITES Appendix II; EU 26 allergen labelling traces; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-3% in fine fragrance for fine-fragrance oud / luxury-niche / sweet-leather accords; preferred Cambodian grade for Western perfumery (vs. heavier Hindi for Middle Eastern market)

odor.description: Sweet, warm, cinnamon-balsamic with soft strawberry-fruity lift and faint honey-coumarinic undertone; defining compound of Basil ct. cinnamate (Thai / Indian basil, 30-60% methyl cinnamate) and Chinese jasmine wax; light, diffusive cinnamon character — less phenolic than cinnamaldehyde, more fruit-honeyed

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 5-8 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone concentration restriction for methyl cinnamate; not a listed IFRA sensitiser; FEMA 2698 GRAS; GHS H317 skin sensitiser (mild); the cinnamate moiety can trigger photo-sensitisation at high dose in UV light but not flagged as phototoxic under IFRA
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance; strawberry / cinnamon / jasmine-adjacent accords; flavouring under FEMA 2698 (strawberry, cherry, grape, honey flavour notes in beverages, confectionery, baked goods); bridges basil cinnamate CT with fruity / balsamic families

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cat.1 = 1%, Cat.2 = 0.25%, Cat.3 = 1.7%, Cat.4 = 6%, Cat.5A = 1.4%, Cat.5B = 0.7%, Cat.5C = 1.4%, Cat.5D = 0.23%, Cat.9 = 3.5%, Cat.10A-B = 3.5%, Cat.11A-B = 1.7%, Cat.12 Not Limited; skin sensitiser H317 (milder than cinnamaldehyde); EU 26 allergen labelling; FEMA 2294 GRAS

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; not on EU 26 allergen list; FEMA 2781 GRAS

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; not on EU 26 allergen list; FEMA 3091 GRAS

odor.description: Sweet, floral, rosy-fruity with soft fresh-lavender and pear-green top; cleaner and less intense than geraniol; characteristic of bergamot/lavandin/geranium accord

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction on geranyl acetate itself; hydrolyzes slowly on skin to geraniol; EU 26 allergen labelling applies via the geraniol moiety (released over time); FEMA 2509 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 1-5% in fine fragrance; natural component of lavandin (3-10%), geranium (5-15%), palmarosa (5-15%), bergamot (0.3-1%), ylang ylang; rounded / less-aggressive alternative to geraniol when soft rosy-citrus effect wanted; not a standalone sensitiser but geraniol disclosure required due to hydrolysis

odor.description: Clean, sweet, powdery-fruity musk with soft balsamic-floral depth; macrocyclic diester — cornerstone of late-1990s environmentally-safer white musk; less tenacious than HHCB but more biodegradable

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; not on EU 26 allergen list; FEMA 3543 GRAS; preferred over polycyclic musks for aquatic toxicity profile
safety.usage: Typical 1-5% in fine fragrance; common workhorse in functional fragrance (detergent / softener); Firmenich trade name Musk T

odor.description: Soft, warm, woody-musky with subtle animalic undertone reminiscent of natural musk; Firmenich macrocyclic lactone (Ruzicka 1926 synthesis); the 'luxury' natural-musk benchmark — very diffuse, very substantive, extremely elegant

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; not on EU 26 allergen list; FEMA 2555 GRAS; ideal environmental + toxicological profile (biodegradable macrocyclic lactone)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance (niche/prestige pushes to 10%+); cost multiplier vs. HHCB but eclipses in elegance; partners beautifully with florals and woods

odor.description: Stronger, rounder, more powerful vanillic character than vanillin; about 3× the odor intensity; slightly less 'natural' but cleaner and more diffusive

odor.description: Sharper, more citrus-herbal than racemic; dominant enantiomer in coriander seed oil; retains light lavender nuance

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — covered under racemic linalool (78-70-6) — FEMA GRAS + IFRA caps non-stereospecific; Cat.1 = 3.8%, Cat.2 = 0.9%, Cat.3 = 5%, Cat.5A = 4.3%, Cat.5B = 2.5%, Cat.5C = 4.3%, Cat.5D = 0.8%, Cat.9 = 13%, Cat.11A-B = 6.7%; Cat.4/10A/10B/12 Not Limited; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (oxidation risk on storage); EU 26 allergen labelling
safety.usage: Treated as linalool by IFRA; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, detergent, candle, air care); FEMA GRAS flavor via linalool FEMA 2635 — the FEMA flavor definition is non-stereospecific for linalool (3,7-dimethyl-1,6-octadien-3-ol), so both enantiomers are covered under the same GRAS listing

odor.description: Sweeter, softer, more floral-lavender than racemic; dominant enantiomer in true lavender; rose-woody nuance

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — covered under racemic linalool (78-70-6) — FEMA GRAS + IFRA caps non-stereospecific; Cat.5A = 4.3% etc. same caps apply; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (oxidation risk on storage); EU 26 allergen labelling
safety.usage: Treated as linalool by IFRA; same Cat.5A 4.3% / Cat.5D 0.8% caps as racemic; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, detergent, candle, air care); FEMA GRAS flavor via linalool FEMA 2635 — the FEMA flavor definition is non-stereospecific for linalool (3,7-dimethyl-1,6-octadien-3-ol), so both enantiomers are covered under the same GRAS listing

odor.description: Soft, sweet, violet-woody with subtle raspberry-rose facet and refined powdery depth; the iconic 'violet' molecule; β-ionone heavier/more woody, α more violet-flowery; discovered by Tiemann 1893

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 + 2023/1545 allergen labelling (α-ionone); FEMA 2594 GRAS (massive berry flavour use); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance; synthesised from citral + acetone (Knoevenagel); cornerstone violet accord + berry-fruity floral

odor.description: Soft, diffusive, orris-violet with characteristic powdery-woody depth and delicate fruity-floral warmth; the workhorse iris substitute since 1893; richer and more substantive than α-ionone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction on methyl ionone (mixed isomers); not on EU 26 allergen list (distinct from simpler ionones); FEMA 2714 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 1-8% in fine fragrance; commercial grades are mixtures of α/β/γ-methyl isomers; cornerstone of iris reconstitution + modern soft-floral powder base

odor.description: Soft, powdery, sweet-violet-orris with characteristic 'fresh-violet-flower' modern signature; EU 26 allergen — labelling required >0.001% (leave-on); the modern industry-standard 'violet molecule' for fine fragrance; commercial blend of 4 isomers (α-isomethyl + α-methyl + β-methyl + γ-methyl ionones); softer + more violet-like than pure α-ionone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — α-isomethyl ionone: cumulative cap with methyl ionone isomers; EU 26 allergen labelling >0.001% leave-on / >0.01% rinse-off; FEMA 2714 GRAS (violet/raspberry flavour); the most-used methyl ionone isomer in modern fragrance
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance for violet-floral / orris-extender / powdery-iris accords; cornerstone of synthetic violet accord (vs. natural violet leaf absolute which is leaf-green)

odor.description: Fresh, dry, pine-woody-resinous with slight minty-eucalyptus lift and terpenic sharpness; less dominant than α-pinene, more woody-foresty

odor.description: Sweet, warm, intense anise-licorice with soft balsamic-floral undertone and characteristic fennel-seed lift; propenyl-isomer cousin of methyl chavicol (allyl isomer); stronger, sweeter, more rounded than estragole; defining compound of Star Anise (~85-90%), Anise (~80-90%), Fennel (~60-75%), Chinese star anise — the canonical 'anise' note in perfumery and flavour

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 5-8 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 restriction — anethole is less concerning than its allyl isomer estragole (lower genotoxic potential) but IFRA still caps to control total exposure: Cat.1 = 0.08%, Cat.2 = 0.23%, Cat.3 = 1%, Cat.4 = 3%, Cat.5A = 0.64%, Cat.5B = 0.44%, Cat.5D = 0.14%, Cat.9 = 0.73%, Cat.10A = 3%, Cat.12 = not restricted; EU 1334/2008 permits anethole as flavouring without ML; FEMA 2086 GRAS; GHS H317 skin sensitiser (via auto-oxidation), H412 aquatic toxicity; less hazardous than methyl chavicol but still sensitiser-managed
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-2% in fine fragrance; anise / licorice / pastis / ouzo accords; classical element in fougère and oriental-gourmand compositions; widely used in oral care (toothpaste, mouthwash) at sweet-licorice flavour levels; food flavouring under FEMA 2086 (confectionery, beverages, baked goods, spirits — Pernod, Sambuca); bridges Basil cinnamate CT and classical anise-family

odor.description: Soft, sweet, creamy-coumarinic with rounded almond-hay character and gentle waxy-lactonic undertone; fully saturated bicyclic lactone — the aromatic ring of parent coumarin is replaced by a cyclohexane, eliminating the photo-/sensitiser-relevant α,β-unsaturated lactone; smoother, gentler coumarin character used as IFRA-friendly modern coumarin replacer in skin-leave applications

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — octahydrocoumarin: not on EU 26 allergen list; FEMA 3791 GRAS for flavour use; designed as low-sensitiser coumarin alternative — saturated lactone scaffold avoids the α,β-unsaturated electrophilic carbonyl that drives parent coumarin's reactivity profile
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for soft coumarinic-creamy-tonka accords; preferred over parent coumarin in skin-leave categories where IFRA Cat.4-5 caps on coumarin become limiting; pairs with tonka, vanilla, hay and gourmand bases

odor.description: Soft, sweet, vegetal-herbal with characteristic celery-lovage warm-soup undertone; partially-saturated phthalide; defining 30-70% volatile of lovage oil + 10-25% of celery seed oil; complex stereoisomer mixture (Z/E + multiple cycloadducts); commercial Ligustilide = natural mixture, structurally unstable on isolation

safety.usage: Typical 0.001-0.05% in fine fragrance — extreme strength + instability mean trace use only; usually delivered as celery/lovage oil rather than isolate

safety.usage: Typical 0.1-3% in fine fragrance as safe oakmoss substitute; modern Chypre construction; pairs with Evernyl + synthetic chypre accord replaces IFRA-restricted oakmoss absolute

odor.description: Sharp, minty-camphoraceous with slightly softer herbal-sage character than α-thujone and faint bitter-wormwood undertone; minor isomer of the thujone pair, present in Dalmatian Sage and Tansy; less neurotoxic than α-thujone but still GABA-A active

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction on the pure compound (restrictions cascade via thujone-bearing oils, e.g. Dalmatian Sage Cat.5A = 0.3%); EU Regulation 1334/2008 flavoring limit in alcoholic beverages; neurotoxic at high concentration though less potent than α-isomer — still a GABA-A antagonist; shares FEMA 3604 with α-thujone
safety.usage: Typical <0.01% in fine fragrance; traces only in sage / wormwood / tansy accords; ⚠ pure β-thujone is a GABA-A antagonist — NOT for leave-on or rinse-off personal care; NOT a standalone food additive (EU Reg 1334/2008 permits thujone only in alcoholic beverages containing sage via natural matrix, not as isolated compound); retained in inventory for trace naturalistic marker use under IFRA-compliant caps only

odor.description: Soft, dry, woody-pine with faint turpentine-amber undertone; major sesquiterpene of Indian longleaf pine (Pinus roxburghii) oleoresin ~20-35%; precursor for perfumery captive Iso E Super+ family

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (tricyclic sesquiterpene — relatively stable); raw material for Amberwood / cedarwood synthetic derivatives
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for dry woody accords; industrial feedstock (converted to isolongifolanone, Iso E Super intermediates)

odor.description: Essentially odorless at use levels; very weak coumarinic-sweet at high concentration on strip; presence detected via UV fluorescence not olfaction

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Bergapten as isolated compound is BANNED in cosmetics under EU Regulation 1223/2009 Annex II #358; permitted in finished consumer product only as natural constituent of citrus peel oils at ≤15 ppm (Cat.1-8, 10-11) or ≤50 ppm (Cat.9 rinse-off short contact); Cat.12 = Not Limited (no skin contact); strongly phototoxic (DNA adduct formation under UVA)
safety.usage: NOT for leave-on or rinse-off personal care as isolate; ⚠ CMR 2 (IARC Group 2B carcinogen, suspected mutagen); reference compound only — isolated bergapten is not used in contemporary perfumery; listed here to anchor the IFRA furanocoumarin cascade applied to bergamot / lime / angelica oils

odor.description: Warm, dry, spicy-anisic-camphoraceous with characteristic dill-fennel-pepper depth and subtle carrot-apiaceae undertone; positional isomer of parsley apiole (different methoxy pattern); defining 5-15% volatile of dill seed oil; rare standalone use; commercial form delivered as part of dill seed oil

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — dill apiole: cumulative cap with apiole + safrole-class methylenedioxy compounds; restricted in cosmetics under EU 1223/2009 Annex II framework; flavour use limited; pregnancy strictly contra-indicated
safety.usage: Typical trace 0.001-0.05% — usually delivered as part of dill seed oil; modern fragrance avoids isolated dill apiole

odor.description: Warm, balsamic-spicy, subtly floral with elemi-gum resinous undertone; delicate phenylpropanoid found in nutmeg (2-4%) and elemi resin (major component)

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; CMR 2 carcinogen concern same as myristicin (phenylpropanoid class); FEMA 3604 GRAS at trace flavouring levels only
safety.usage: Trace use only; normally encountered via nutmeg / elemi matrix; research reference for resinous warmth

odor.description: Warm, floral-fruity, celery-herbaceous jasmine top note with distinctive pale-honey nuance; the 'green jasmine' character above the indole

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; mild skin irritant at very high concentration; FEMA 3196 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance; signature green-floral component of jasmine (2-3%), osmanthus, bergamot (trace); preferred trans-jasmone grades for cleaner character

odor.description: Soft, warm, saffron-tobacco with characteristic hay-leather sweet-balsamic undertone; minor (~1-3%) saffron stigma volatile but olfactively significant; the 'saffron-warmth' molecule complementing safranal's sharper top; key saffron-accord component

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

odor.description: Sharp, sweet, peppermint-camphoraceous with minty-floral undertone and faint pulegone-cooling depth; biosynthetic precursor of Pulegone in the peppermint biosynthetic pathway (Piperitenone → Piperitone → Pulegone → Menthone → Menthol); minor natural component of various Mentha species (M. piperita, M. arvensis, M. pulegium)

safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.2% in fine fragrance for mint biosynthetic-authentic / peppermint-floral accords; flavour at trace ppm; primarily research / aromatic chemistry interest as menthol biosynthesis precursor

safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance when isolated; dominant component of aged patchouli oil odor; pairs with iso-E-super / ambroxan for modern woody accords

odor.description: Fresh, zesty, sweet-citrus orange-peel with clean herbaceous-woody undertone; defining citrus terpene, also present in rosemary / pine / conifer

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — peroxide value ≤20 mmol/L as sold and ≤0.09% in finished product (hydroperoxides drive sensitisation); EU 26 allergen (limonene — extended labelling via 2023/1545 on auto-oxidised limonene); store cool/dark under inert gas; GHS H226 flammable, H304 aspiration hazard, H315 skin irritation, H317 skin sensitiser via hydroperoxides, H410 aquatic toxicity; FEMA 2633 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; ubiquitous in citrus / herbal / clean accords; one of the most-used fragrance ingredients by volume; peroxide control and EU 26 allergen labelling mandatory

odor.description: Soft, mild, honeyed-floral rose-like with light wine-spirit undertone; gentle and diffusive; workhorse rose reconstitution material used in virtually every floral composition

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; mild skin irritant at very high concentration; FEMA 2858 GRAS; not on EU 26 allergen list (large molecular weight + low sensitising potential); stable — one of the few rose materials that doesn't require BHT stabilisation
safety.usage: Typical 2-20% in fine fragrance for rose-honey-waxy body; 50-70% of rose absolute; produced industrially by hydrogenation of styrene oxide or ethylene + benzene; cornerstone of synthetic rose / jasmine / lily accords

odor.description: Soft, earthy-creamy with floral-woody undertone and faint camphoraceous lift; 6-membered tetrahydropyran ring form of linalool oxide — denser and more earthy than the furanoid isomer, lower volatility, more creamy-woody body; minor linalool autoxidation product found in aged lavender / bergamot / rose oils and in some naturally oxidised teas (black tea, oolong)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone concentration restriction; GHS H315 skin irritation, H319 eye irritation; FEMA 3746 GRAS (shared with furanoid isomer); peroxide formation similar to parent linalool — store cool/dark
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.1% in fine fragrance; used as trace-level earthy-woody modifier in tea / aged-lavender / chypre accords; food flavouring under FEMA 3746 (GRAS covers both furanoid and pyranoid linalool oxide forms)

odor.description: Warm, savoury-celery-spicy with characteristic 'lovage-bouillon' meaty-soup depth; minor (~1-5%) phthalide-class compound of lovage root oil + Ligusticum chuanxiong (Chinese lovage); chemotaxonomic identifier of premium lovage chemotype; rare commercial standalone — usually delivered as part of lovage root oil

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — sedanonic anhydride: cumulative cap with lovage + celery phthalides; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; light + air sensitive (rapid hydrolysis to sedanonic acid + dimerization); not on EU 26 allergen list; pregnancy contra-indicated
safety.usage: Typical trace 0.001-0.05% — chemotaxonomic identifier; usually delivered as part of premium lovage root oil rather than isolate (commercially rare)

odor.description: Strong, sweet-celery, warm-vegetal with characteristic celery-soup-stalk soft-fatty-green undertone; defining 5-30% molecule of celery seed oil; iconic phthalide olfactive marker; extremely powerful — usable at trace levels

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — phthalides: cumulative cap with celery seed oil; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; not on EU 26 allergen list; FEMA 3334 GRAS (celery flavour use); pharmaceutical interest (anti-stroke agent in TCM)
safety.usage: Typical 0.001-0.1% in fine fragrance — extreme strength means trace use; defining celery-vegetal accord molecule; pairs with green hexenols + aldehydes for soup-vegetable effects

safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.1% when isolated; almost always encountered via nutmeg / mace oil matrix; not standalone in modern fragrance — oil form preferred

performance.tenacity: High — cooling effect 3-6 hours on skin / 1-2 hours mucosa

odor.description: Fresh, herbaceous, camphoraceous-pine with eucalyptol-led cool-medicinal top and faint woody-resinous undertone; chemotype-dependent character (ct.cineole cool-medicinal, ct.camphor sharp-menthol, ct.verbenone soft-green)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry for R. officinalis; caps cascade from 1,8-cineole (~20-55%), camphor (~10-25%), and α-pinene (~10-20%) content — Cat.5A / Cat.5B/Cat.5D driven by camphor fraction (Cat.5A ≤ 3.75% assuming 25% camphor); ct.verbenone (low-camphor) permits higher leave-on caps; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + limonene traces)

odor.description: Dry, smooth, pencil-shaving woody with soft sweet-balsamic warmth; clean character dominated by cedrol + α-cedrene + thujopsene; American red cedar is functional perfumery workhorse

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Juniperus virginiana wood oil: Cat.1-12 no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen labelling generally not required; FEMA 2263 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 1-10% in fine fragrance for dry-woody base; cornerstone of functional perfumery (soap / detergent); pairs with vetiver / patchouli / sandalwood

odor.description: Fresh, sweet, floral-herbal with soft camphoraceous undertone and balsamic depth; characteristic of true lavender

safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; Cat.12 not limited; natural allergen disclosure required when linalool >10 ppm leave-on / >100 ppm rinse-off; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, detergent, candle, air care)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Syzygium aromaticum bud oil: Cat.1 = 0.02%, Cat.2 = 0.005%, Cat.3 = 0.04%, Cat.4 = 0.15%, Cat.5A = 0.03%, Cat.5B = 0.02%, Cat.5C = 0.03%, Cat.5D = 0.006%, Cat.9 = 0.24%, Cat.10A-B = 0.24%, Cat.11A-B = 0.1%, Cat.12 Not Limited; eugenol-driven QRA2 cascade (75-85% eugenol, skin sensitiser H317); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Carum carvi seed oil: cumulative cap with carvone class; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2238 GRAS (rye-bread/liqueur flavour); pregnancy contra-indicated at high dose

odor.description: Fresh, green-rosy, minty-herbaceous with characteristic citronellol-geraniol rose-pelargonium depth and subtle cedar-mint top; the affordable 'poor man's rose' — indispensable rose extender; Reunion Island Bourbon chemotype = industry gold standard

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Pelargonium graveolens oil: no specific concentration restriction on oil; EU 26 + 2023/1545 allergen labelling (citronellol + geraniol + linalool); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2508 GRAS

odor.description: Cool, sharp, medicinal-camphoraceous with clean eucalyptol-led top and faint woody-balsamic undertone; defining Eucalyptus globulus profile — 70-85% 1,8-cineole drives the fresh medicinal character, α-pinene and α-terpineol add lift and softness

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry; caps cascade from 1,8-cineole (~70-85%) content — no concentration restriction on cineole per se but peroxide ≤20 mmol/L for the oil; EU 26 allergen (limonene traces); concentrated inhalation can trigger respiratory irritation — avoid high Cat.5D baby-product doses
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-2% in fine fragrance; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, candle, air care, cold/cough rubs); food flavouring under FEMA 2466 at low ppm (cough drops, mouthwash, minty-medicinal accords); industrial use far exceeds fragrance use (aromatherapy, pharma, cleaning)

odor.description: Sparkling, fresh, cool-spicy with complex eucalyptus-camphoraceous top and warm terpinyl-acetate floral-spicy heart; dominated by α-terpinyl acetate (30-40%) + 1,8-cineole (25-30%) with sabinene / linalool modifiers; the classical fresh-spicy bridge between fresh and oriental families

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Elettaria cardamomum fruit oil: no specific concentration restriction on oil (linalool + limonene content drive labelling); EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + limonene + geraniol traces); not phototoxic (distillate); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (monoterpene content); FEMA 2241 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.2-2% in fine fragrance for sparkling fresh-spicy top; Guatemalan + Indian (Malabar / Mysore) origins; CO2 extract retains more volatile freshness than steam distillate

odor.description: Rich, spicy-floral, warm-clove-rosy with characteristic eugenol-isoeugenol-benzyl-benzoate signature; defining 'spicy carnation' floral; very rare commercial natural (~$20000/kg) — most modern carnation accords are synthetic; iconic Caron Bellodgia signature material; classical floral-spice cornerstone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Dianthus caryophyllus absolute: cumulative cap with eugenol + isoeugenol; EU 26 allergen labelling (eugenol + isoeugenol + benzyl benzoate); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; sensitizer (eugenol)

odor.description: Sharp, warm, spicy-herbaceous with characteristic phenolic-anise apiole-myristicin signature; rich, dry-resinous green-pepper undertone vs. fresh leaf chemotype; defining 20-50% apiole + 10-30% myristicin profile; iconic Mediterranean-spice material; restricted in cosmetics due to apiole content

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

odor.description: Rich, dark, roasted-coffee with characteristic bitter-burnt pyrazine / furanone / mercaptan depth and subtle chocolate-caramel undertone; 800+ identified aroma molecules; defining gourmand-dark-café accord

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cananga odorata flower oil: Cat.1 = 0.8%, Cat.2 = 0.2%, Cat.3 = 1.2%, Cat.4 = 4.0%, Cat.5A = 1.0%, Cat.5B = 0.5%, Cat.5C = 1.0%, Cat.5D = 0.15%, Cat.9 = 5.0%, Cat.10A-B = 5.0%, Cat.11A-B = 2.5%, Cat.12 Not Limited; QRA2 skin-sensitiser caps driven by isoeugenol + benzyl salicylate + linalool content; EU 26 / 2023/1545 allergen labelling (benzyl benzoate + benzyl salicylate + linalool + geraniol + isoeugenol + farnesol); FEMA 3119 GRAS

odor.description: Dry, sharp, warm-spicy with fresh terpenic-woody top and faint citrus-peppery bite; anchored by β-caryophyllene + sabinene + limonene

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Piper nigrum fruit oil: no standalone cap (mild terpene profile, no EU 26 major allergens); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (high β-caryophyllene + sabinene, autoxidation risk); FEMA 2845 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-0.5% in fine fragrance for spicy-masculine accords; central to modern Chypre / Fougère freshness; pairs with vetiver / patchouli / cardamom

odor.description: Sharp, herbaceous, camphoraceous-warm with characteristic pinocamphone-driven minty bite and pine-resinous undertone; classic European herbal — Hyssopus officinalis flowering tops give a 30-50% pinocamphone + isopinocamphone profile that distinguishes hyssop from sage and rosemary; medicinal-aromatic with subtle eucalyptol freshness

odor.description: Sweet, warm, anise-licorice with soft camphoraceous lift and green-spicy undertone; smoother and rounder than bitter fennel due to higher anethole / lower fenchone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Foeniculum vulgare var. dulce oil: Cat.1 = 0.08%, Cat.2 = 0.08%, Cat.3 = 0.5%, Cat.4 = 1.4%, Cat.5A = 0.4%, Cat.5B = 0.2%, Cat.5C = 0.4%, Cat.5D = 0.07%, Cat.9 = 2.5%, Cat.10A-B = 2.5%, Cat.11A-B = 1.2%, Cat.12 Not Limited; estragole-driven restriction (3-6% methyl chavicol, CMR 2 genotoxic carcinogen); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L

odor.description: Rich, warm, creamy-woody with balsamic-milky depth and soft floral-animalic undertone; quintessential sandalwood character anchored by 40-55% α-santalol + 20-30% β-santalol; classical Indian perfumery foundation

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Santalum album oil: Cat.1 = 1.5%, Cat.2 = 0.35%, Cat.3 = 2.4%, Cat.4 = 9%, Cat.5A = 2%, Cat.5B = 1%, Cat.5C = 2%, Cat.5D = 0.35%, Cat.9 = 11%, Cat.10A-B = 11%, Cat.11A-B = 5.5%, Cat.12 Not Limited; FEMA 3005 GRAS; CITES Appendix II listed — sustainable sourcing required (Santalum album has been over-harvested)

odor.description: Fresh, intensely cooling, camphoraceous-herbaceous with characteristic menthol-menthone dominated top and grassy-minty depth; l-menthol 35-50% + l-menthone 15-30% + menthyl acetate 4-10% drive the cooling-fresh character

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Mentha piperita oil: no specific concentration restriction on oil; EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene + linalool traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2848 GRAS (major flavour use)
