odor.description: Deep, sweet, cinnamon-vanilla with characteristic 'leather-balsamic' rich-resinous depth and subtle smoky-tobacco undertone; defining benzyl cinnamate (~50%) + benzyl benzoate; classical balsamic + iconic skin sensitizer (Mexican Mickey Mouse Balsam)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Myroxylon balsamum pereirae: PROHIBITED in cosmetics — strong skin sensitizer (EU Regulation 1223/2009 Annex II); flavor use only with strict labelling; FEMA 3035 GRAS at trace
safety.usage: RESTRICTED — modern fragrance uses synthetic substitutes; classical reference material only

odor.description: Deep, rich, sweet-floral rosy with honey-citrus lift and subtle spicy-balsamic undertone; complex citronellol-geraniol core with β-damascenone and rose-oxide green-fruity accents; THE reference rose accord

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Rosa damascena flower oil: Cat.1 = 1.7%, Cat.2 = 0.4%, Cat.3 = 2.6%, Cat.4 = 9%, Cat.5A = 2.2%, Cat.5B = 1.1%, Cat.5C = 2.2%, Cat.5D = 0.35%, Cat.9 = 5%, Cat.10A-B = 5%, Cat.11A-B = 2.5%, Cat.12 Not Limited; driven by allergen load (citronellol 30-40%, geraniol 15-25%, eugenol 0.5-2% — EU 26); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-3% in fine fragrance for heart notes; FEMA 2988 GRAS; central to Chypre / Floral / Oriental families; rose otto (steam-distilled) has sharper lemony lift than rose absolute

odor.description: Fresh, warm, pungent-spicy with sparkling lemon-terpenic top and characteristic gingery-resinous woody depth; sesquiterpene-dominated (α-zingiberene 25-40%, β-sesquiphellandrene 10-15%, ar-curcumene 5-10%) with citral top; note the non-pungent distillate — gingerol/shogaol stay in oleoresin

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Zingiber officinale rhizome oil: no specific concentration restriction on oil (citral content drives labelling); EU 26 allergen labelling (citral + geraniol + linalool + limonene); not phototoxic (distillate); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (monoterpene content); FEMA 2521 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.2-2% in fine fragrance for fresh-spicy top/heart; Indian + Chinese + Nigerian origins; CO2 extract preserves more zingiberene than steam distillate; essential for modern sparkling gourmand and fresh-oriental accords

odor.description: Strong, sharp, spicy-phenolic with warm herbal-camphoraceous body and dry balsamic undertone; carvacrol-dominant thyme sibling

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry; caps cascade from carvacrol (~60-75%) and thymol (~5-15%) content: Cat.1 = 0.15%, Cat.2 = 0.44%, Cat.3 = 1.87%, Cat.4 = 5.33%, Cat.5A = 1.2%, Cat.5B = 0.8%, Cat.5D = 0.27%, Cat.9 = 1.33%, Cat.10A = 5.33% (conservative ceilings assuming 65% carvacrol — batch COA drives actual cap); EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool traces)
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.3% in fine fragrance; phenol-rich dermally sensitising — avoid high leave-on; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, detergent, herbal care) and food flavouring under FEMA 2660

odor.description: More delicate, drier, and more 'lifted' than nutmeg; less heavy phenylpropanoid depth; similar monoterpene fingerprint but cleaner sweet-spicy profile; preferred for elegant spicy accords

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Myristica fragrans aril (mace) oil: caps parallel to nutmeg oil (Cat.4 ≈ 0.18%); myristicin + elemicin content similar; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2653 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.02-0.15% in fine fragrance; more expensive than nutmeg oil; preferred for refined spicy accords where lighter character wanted

odor.description: Sharp, fresh, camphoraceous-medicinal with bitter-sage and wormwood undertone and dry coniferous-resinous body; richest commercial source of α-thujone (48-65%) — defining note of naturalistic vintage / chypre / absinthe-recall accords

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil cap but cascades heavily from α-thujone (48-65%) + β-thujone (8-15%) content: Cat.5A ≤ 0.15%, Cat.5B ≤ 0.1%, Cat.5D ≤ 0.03% (conservative baby-product ceiling); EU Regulation 1334/2008 prohibits use in flavouring except alcoholic beverages with sage; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2267 GRAS only for historical alcoholic beverages use (bitters, vermouth)
safety.usage: Typical <0.05% in fine fragrance for trace naturalistic marker use; neurotoxic at high concentration (thujone is GABA-A antagonist) — AVOID leave-on cosmetics; historical flavouring (bitters, absinthe revival products) only under EU 1334/2008; functional perfumery limited to candle / incense where skin exposure is minimal

odor.description: Strong, sharp, intensely-minty-camphoraceous with characteristic 'hot-pulegone' burnt-mint pungent character; defining 60-90% (R)-(+)-pulegone; classical insect-repellent + traditional medicine; HEAVILY RESTRICTED in modern fragrance + flavour due to pulegone hepatotoxicity (EU Regulation 1223/2009 Annex II flag); iconic but dangerous

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 3-5 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Mentha pulegium oil: PROHIBITED in cosmetics (EU Regulation 1223/2009 Annex II — pulegone hepatotoxicity); flavor use ≤20 ppm pulegone; pregnancy strictly contra-indicated; multiple historical fatalities at high dose
safety.usage: RESTRICTED — limited to flavour use at <20 ppm pulegone; modern fragrance avoids; classical reference material only

odor.description: Powerful, warm, sharp-medicinal, phenolic-herbal with spicy-camphoraceous depth and a dry woody-balsamic undertone; defining thymol-type thyme

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry; caps cascade from thymol (~35-55%) and carvacrol (~1-10%) content: Cat.1 = 0.13%, Cat.2 = 0.4%, Cat.3 = 1.6%, Cat.4 = 4.8%, Cat.5A = 1%, Cat.5B = 0.72%, Cat.5D = 0.22%, Cat.9 = 1.24%, Cat.10A = 4.8% (conservative ceilings assuming 45% thymol — batch COA phenol % drives actual cap); EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool traces)
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.3% in fine fragrance; phenol-rich dermally sensitising — avoid Cat.5D leave-on baby products beyond 0.1%; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, detergent, herbal personal care) and food-flavour compounding under FEMA 3063

odor.description: Fresh, clean, camphoraceous-eucalyptolic with herbaceous-floral nuance and slight peppery undertone; defining Laurus nobilis profile — 30-50% 1,8-cineole drives the eucalyptol-fresh top, with sabinene + α-pinene + linalool + terpinen-4-ol building a softer, less medicinal profile than eucalyptus globulus; classic Mediterranean culinary + perfumery herb

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — bay laurel: caps cascade from cineole + sabinene; methyleugenol trace concern in some chemotypes (restricted); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen (linalool, eugenol traces)
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-0.5% in fine fragrance for Mediterranean herbal / fougère accords; widely used in food flavour (FEMA 2125) at low ppm; pomade base, pharma, aromatherapy

odor.description: Warm, sweet, anisic-licorice with soft coumarinic undertone and faint camphor lift; smoother and more delicate than star anise, with a fennel-adjacent character

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Pimpinella anisum oil: Cat.1 = 0.5%, Cat.2 = 0.5%, Cat.3 = 1.6%, Cat.4 = 3.3%, Cat.5A = 1%, Cat.5B = 0.5%, Cat.5C = 1%, Cat.5D = 0.2%, Cat.9 = 5%, Cat.10A-B = 5%, Cat.11A-B = 2.5%, Cat.12 Not Limited; estragole content typically 1-3% (lower than fennel); trans-anethole 85-90% — large oral doses can trigger seizures in sensitive individuals; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-0.5% in fine fragrance for clean anise-licorice character; FEMA 2094 GRAS; use true Pimpinella anisum oil — don't confuse with star anise or Japanese star anise (Illicium anisatum — toxic)

odor.description: Fresh, sweet, citrus with distinctive floral-fruity nuance; rich linalool-ester character over a crisp limonene top; slight balsamic undertone from coumarinic trace fraction

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus bergamia peel oil (expressed): Cat.1 = 0.4%, Cat.2 = 0.4%, Cat.3 = 0.4%, Cat.4 = 0.4%, Cat.5A = 0.4%, Cat.5B = 0.4%, Cat.5C = 0.4%, Cat.5D = 0.4%, Cat.6 = 0.4%, Cat.7A = 0.4%, Cat.7B = 0.4%, Cat.8 = 0.4%, Cat.9 = 4%, Cat.10A = 0.4%, Cat.10B = 0.4%, Cat.11A = 0.4%, Cat.11B = 0.4%, Cat.12 Not Limited; phototoxic (furanocoumarin-driven — bergapten ≤15 ppm in finished consumer product); EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + limonene)
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-0.4% in fine fragrance for leave-on; up to 4% in rinse-off (Cat.9); iconic Cologne / Fougère / Chypre top note; use FCF version (89957-91-5) when phototoxic cap is limiting or sun-contact formulation required

odor.description: Sharper, drier, more aldehyde-forward than Ceylon cinnamon bark; cinnamaldehyde 75-90% with very low eugenol; harsher character favored in gourmand bakery accords (colas, cinnamon rolls)

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cinnamomum cassia oil: slightly tighter caps than C. verum due to higher cinnamaldehyde content; Cat.4 = 0.04%, Cat.5A = 0.008%; FEMA 2258 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.005-0.04% in fine fragrance; dominant in flavour work (cola, baked-goods) rather than perfumery; common source of commercial cinnamaldehyde isolation

odor.description: Sharp, fresh, slightly-medicinal-citrus with characteristic 'cool-lime' green-piney undertone; defining 50% d-limonene + 5-15% β-pinene + 5-10% γ-terpinene + 1-3% terpinen-4-ol; cold-pressed = phototoxic + brighter; distilled = less harsh + flatter

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus aurantifolia expressed: STRONGLY phototoxic; Cat.4 = 0.7% leave-on / Cat.5A = 0.2%; distilled grade = no phototoxicity restriction; FEMA 2632 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; cold-pressed for sparkle (capped) or distilled for unrestricted blending

odor.description: Soft, sweet, slightly-floral-citrus with characteristic 'fruity-mandarin' methyl-N-methyl-anthranilate green undertone; defining 65-75% d-limonene + 1-2% methyl-N-methyl-anthranilate (the green-floral signature); softer + more rounded than orange; Italian green/red grades

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus reticulata oil: phototoxicity Cat.4 = 4.0% (lower vs. lemon/lime); FEMA 2657 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 1-8% in fine fragrance for soft-citrus / fruity-citrus / Italian-cologne accords

odor.description: Warm, sweet-spicy with fresh terpenic-pine top and characteristic myristicin resinous-nutty depth; complex monoterpene profile (sabinene 15-30%, α-pinene 15-25%) + phenylpropanoids; classical spicy accord cornerstone

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 5-8 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Myristica fragrans seed oil: Cat.1 = 0.03%, Cat.2 = 0.007%, Cat.3 = 0.05%, Cat.4 = 0.18%, Cat.5A = 0.04%, Cat.5B = 0.02%, Cat.5C = 0.04%, Cat.5D = 0.007%, Cat.9 = 0.25%, Cat.10A-B = 0.25%, Cat.11A-B = 0.12%, Cat.12 Not Limited; myristicin / safrole CMR 2 restrictions drive cap; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (high monoterpene content); FEMA 2793 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.02-0.2% in fine fragrance for warm-spicy accords; East Indian (Banda Islands) is benchmark; West Indian (Grenada) slightly lighter in character

odor.description: Sharp, fresh, camphoraceous-medicinal with characteristic 'mothball-pharmacy' camphoraceous-piney undertone; defining ~50% camphor + 1,8-cineole + safrole (chemotype-dependent — WHITE FRACTION ONLY for cosmetics; brown/yellow safrole-rich are PROHIBITED); iconic Asian classical material; natural source of camphor isolate (vs. synthetic from α-pinene)

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-5 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cinnamomum camphora oil (white): cumulative cap with camphor + 1,8-cineole; safrole-bearing fractions PROHIBITED in EU cosmetics (yellow/brown camphor); white camphor (cineole-camphor fraction) only permitted; FEMA 2230 GRAS at trace; pregnancy contra-indicated
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for camphor / Asian-medicinal / classical-mothball accords; modern fragrance prefers synthetic camphor isolate (cleaner profile without safrole regulatory issues)

odor.description: Fresh, sweet-woody, spicy-floral with characteristic (S)-(+)-linalool dominated heart (65-80%) and delicate citrusy-peppery top; seed oil only — leaf ('cilantro') oil is aldehydic-soapy entirely different

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Coriandrum sativum fruit (seed) oil: no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool 65-80% + limonene traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2334 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for fresh-spicy floral accords; Russian + Romanian origins; cornerstone linalool-rich natural (alongside ho wood)

odor.description: Sharp, fresh, sparkling-citrus with characteristic 'lemon-zest' bright-acid undertone; defining 60-70% d-limonene + 5-15% β-pinene + 1-3% citral; iconic Eau de Cologne cornerstone (4711, Eau de Cologne Impériale); Sicilian/Argentine grades preferred

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus limon oil expressed: phototoxic furanocoumarin (bergapten + oxypeucedanin); Cat.4 = 2.0%, Cat.5A = 0.5% leave-on; FEMA 2625 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (rapid air-oxidation)
safety.usage: Typical 1-10% in Eau de Cologne; cold-pressed expressed = phototoxic, distilled (terpeneless) = unrestricted

safety.ifra: Awaiting curation — treat as restricted. Consult IFRA 51 + CosIng directly before use. (Auto-generated stub from PubChem.)
safety.usage: No curated usage range — start <0.1% in product and review against IFRA 51 / RIFM safety files before scaling.

odor.description: Sweet, warm, minty-fruity with characteristic (R)-(-)-carvone signature and soft limonene top; l-carvone 50-70% drives the sweet chewing-gum character; NOT cooling (lacks menthol)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Mentha spicata oil: no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 3032 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.2-2% in fine fragrance; Native + Scotch spearmint distinct chemotypes; American Midwest origin dominant

odor.description: Powerful, bitter-herbaceous-camphoraceous with characteristic absinthe-thujone signature; sharp, dry, slightly anisic-medicinal undertone; defining 35-50% α-thujone + 5-15% β-thujone profile; iconic absinthe / vermouth bitter-herb spirit material; strong + uncompromising

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Artemisia absinthium oil: PROHIBITED in fine fragrance + cosmetics (EU Regulation 1223/2009 Annex II) due to thujone toxicity; thujone-free reconstitutions permitted; FEMA 3116 GRAS at low ppm in alcoholic beverages only
safety.usage: RESTRICTED — flavour use only at <10 ppm thujone (alcoholic beverages); modern perfumery uses thujone-free 'wormwood accord' or low-thujone Mediterranean chemotypes only

odor.description: Sharp, fresh, eucalyptus-camphor with characteristic medicinal-piney undertone and subtle slightly-sweeter character vs. eucalyptus; ~40-65% 1,8-cineole + α-pinene + α-terpineol; classical Indonesian + Vietnamese medicinal oil; foundational for tropical medicinal accords + functional fragrance

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 3-6 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Melaleuca cajuputi oil: cumulative cap with cineole oils; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2225 GRAS; skin sensitizer at high concentration
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-1% in fine fragrance for medicinal-eucalyptus / Indonesian-medicinal / functional accords

safety.ifra: Awaiting curation — treat as restricted. Consult IFRA 51 + CosIng directly before use. (Auto-generated stub from PubChem.)
safety.usage: No curated usage range — start <0.1% in product and review against IFRA 51 / RIFM safety files before scaling.

odor.description: Soft, sweet, honey-waxy with characteristic 'honeycomb-pollen' powdery-leathery undertone and subtle floral depth; defining beeswax fixative material; iconic Soft Amber cornerstone (Guerlain Plus Que Jamais, Serge Lutens Miel de Bois)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cera alba absolute: not on EU 26 allergen list; sustainability-considered (apiculture)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance for honey / waxy / Soft Amber niche accords

odor.description: Warm, smoky-tarry, leathery-phenolic with characteristic prickly-juniper wood-charcoal depth; dry-distillation product from Juniperus oxycedrus wood; cornerstone of classical 'cuir' (leather) base accords

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Juniperus oxycedrus wood oil (rectified): Cat.1 = 0.03%, Cat.2 = 0.007%, Cat.3 = 0.05%, Cat.4 = 0.15%, Cat.5A = 0.04%, Cat.5B = 0.02%, Cat.5C = 0.04%, Cat.5D = 0.006%, Cat.9 = 0.2%, Cat.10A-B = 0.2%, Cat.11A-B = 0.1%, Cat.12 Not Limited; rectified grade ONLY permitted (crude contains benzo[a]pyrene + PAH contamination > IFRA limits); FEMA 2263 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.02-0.15% in fine fragrance for leather / smoky / incense base accords; crude cade oil IFRA-prohibited in fine fragrance

odor.description: Sharp, sweet, marzipan-cherry with characteristic 'almond-extract' nutty-cherry-pie undertone; defining 95-99% benzaldehyde + traces hydrocyanic acid (commercial supply: hydrogen-cyanide-free); iconic almond accord cornerstone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Prunus amygdalus amara oil: HCN-free grade required (raw oil contains toxic HCN); FEMA 2046 GRAS at trace; benzaldehyde primary olfactive component
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for almond / cherry-pie / heliotrope accords

odor.description: Fresh, dry, balsamic-coniferous with characteristic 'Tuscan-cypress' resinous-pine-juniper undertone and subtle leathery depth; defining 50% α-pinene + 5-15% Δ-3-carene + 5-10% cedrol + cedrene; iconic Mediterranean coniferous wood; modern niche material (Carthusia Mediterraneo, Maison Margiela Whispers in the Library)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cupressus sempervirens oil: peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (rapid α-pinene oxidation); EU 26 allergen labelling traces; cumulative cap with monoterpene hydrocarbons
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance for cypress / Mediterranean / coniferous-niche accords

odor.description: Deep, earthy, camphoraceous-woody with distinct rich sweet-balsamic depth and faint animalic undertone; improves dramatically with aging (1-5 years); Indonesian (Sumatra) is benchmark grade

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction on Pogostemon cablin oil; EU 26 allergen labelling may apply at high inclusion levels; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (seco-guaianes autoxidise); FEMA 2838 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; cornerstone of Chypre / Oriental / Fougère bases; dominant molecule is patchoulol (30-40%); rich in norpatchoulenol (trace but odor-defining); matures dramatically on aging — iron-stained aged oil is perfumery standard

odor.description: Intensely warm, sweaty-spicy, curry-animalic with characteristic cuminaldehyde (25-40%) signature and green-fatty undertone; distinct 'body-odor' facet at overdose — use with restraint; ME/North African cuisine cornerstone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cuminum cyminum oil: no specific concentration restriction on oil; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (aldehyde content); EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene traces); potentially photosensitising at high doses (aromatic aldehyde concern); FEMA 2343 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.3% in fine fragrance — extremely potent, use sparingly; provides animalic-spicy warmth to oriental / gourmand / spicy compositions

odor.description: Fresh, bitter-green, citrus-herbaceous with characteristic linalyl-acetate softness over bitter peel-leaf top; drier and greener than neroli; essential Cologne freshness

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus aurantium leaf oil: no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + limonene); not phototoxic (distillate); FEMA 2855 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance for bright top notes; Paraguay origin most common; pairs with bergamot + neroli for classical eau de cologne trio

odor.description: Fresh, camphoraceous-medicinal with characteristic 'eucalyptus-cineole' clean undertone and subtle viridiflorol-balsamic depth; ~50-60% 1,8-cineole + viridiflorol + α-terpineol + β-caryophyllene; New Caledonian + Madagascan medicinal oil; classical aromatherapy + perfumery use

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Melaleuca quinquenervia oil: cumulative cap with cineole oils; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene + traces); skin sensitizer at high concentration
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-1% in fine fragrance for medicinal-eucalyptus / fresh-aromatic / niche-natural accords

odor.description: Soft, sweet, lemony-herbal with honeyed-rosaceous undertone and gentle citral-driven freshness; defining Melissa officinalis profile — 30-50% citral (geranial + neral) with citronellal + β-caryophyllene + geraniol; uniquely 'lemon-Lamiaceae' character bridging citrus and herbal — far gentler than lemongrass; commercial supply often adulterated due to extremely low yield (~0.01-0.1% from leaf)

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — citral-rich (Cat.4 ≈ 0.6% leave-on for sensitiser dose); EU 26 allergen disclosure (citral, citronellol, geraniol, linalool); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; commercial market frequently adulterated with citronella / lemongrass / litsea
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.3% in fine fragrance for lemon-honey-herbal accords; aromatherapy + pharma (anxiolytic / sedative claims); ultra-premium commercial supply (~$2000-4000/kg authentic) due to low oil yield

odor.description: Warm, soft, sweet-herbaceous with pine-woody undertone and faint spicy-peppery lift; terpinen-4-ol + linalool + cis-sabinene hydrate chemotype — distinct from oregano (O. vulgare carvacrol-dominant); gentle, lamb-roast-adjacent culinary character; bridges thyme and pine facet families

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry for O. majorana; far milder than O. vulgare (oregano) because carvacrol is low (<5%) while terpinen-4-ol (20-35%) dominates; no phenolic-sensitisation risk at typical levels; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool ~20%)
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance; provençal herbs / herbes de provence accords, soft culinary-herbal compositions; food flavouring under FEMA 2655 (lamb, roasted meats, spice blends); functional perfumery (soap) where soft thyme character is wanted

odor.description: Warm, sweet, musky-animalic with delicate brandy-nut top and rich macrocyclic musk heart; dominated by (Z)-ambrettolide (7-15%) + (Z)-7-hexadecenolide; the principal botanical natural musk since whale/deer musks phase-out (alongside ambergris and hyraceum on the animal side)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Abelmoschus moschatus seed oil: no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen labelling (farnesol); FEMA 2016 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance (extremely expensive — Indian origin); irreplaceable natural animalic-musk for niche; CO2 extract preferred over steam distillate

odor.description: Warm, sweet, aromatic-herbaceous with anise-peppery lift and soft floral-green undertone; chemotype-dependent — European (linalool CT, 30-55% linalool) is soft-floral-herbal; Exotic/Comoros (methyl chavicol CT, 70-85% estragole) is sharp anise-herbaceous; Egyptian / French (balanced) sits between the two; eugenol (0-10%) adds clove-warmth; methyl cinnamate (trace-5%) adds faint cinnamon lift

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry for O. basilicum; caps cascade from methyl chavicol (estragole, up to 85% in Exotic CT) and methyl eugenol (2-15% in some CTs) content — genotoxic-carcinogen concern drives tight restriction: Cat.5A ≤ 0.01% for Exotic CT, Cat.4 ≤ 0.1%, Cat.5D ≤ 0.002% (conservative baby-product ceiling); European/linalool CT much safer (up to Cat.5A ≤ 1%); EU 1334/2008 restricts estragole + methyl eugenol in flavouring; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool, limonene, eugenol traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-1% in fine fragrance; chemotype selection critical — European linalool CT preferred for leave-on / fine fragrance; Exotic methyl-chavicol CT reserved for functional / rinse-off / flavouring where exposure is limited; food flavouring under FEMA 2119 (herbes de provence, pesto base, Italian / Thai cuisine) at low ppm; batch COA chemotype % determines actual cap

odor.description: Sweet, soft, floral-woody with rosy-lavender linalool character and warm balsamic undertone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific restriction; CITES Appendix II (endangered) — sourcing restricted; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool ~85%); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; store cool/dark — linalool autoxidation produces allergenic hydroperoxides
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; sustainable alternatives preferred due to CITES listing — see Ho Wood Oil

odor.description: Warm, dry, woody-rooty with characteristic earthy-violet-orris carotol-driven depth and subtle spicy-iris undertone; defining 30-70% carotol + 10-25% daucol sesquiterpene-alcohol profile; iconic 'rooty-earthy' niche material; modern Hermès Eau d'Hermès / Eau d'Orange Verte / Aedes de Venustas signature

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Daucus carota seed oil: no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2244 GRAS; pregnancy contra-indicated (uterine stimulant per traditional medicine)
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for woody-rooty / iris-orris-violet alternative / earthy-niche accords; cheaper iris substitute (carotol mimics ironone-warmth)

odor.description: Sweet, warm-spicy, herbaceous-celery with characteristic phthalide-rich (3-butylphthalide + ligustilide) defining 'celery soup' signature; rich in d-limonene (~60%) + β-selinene + sedanolides; iconic culinary-fragrance herb material; modern niche perfumery use for green-vegetal accord

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Apium graveolens seed oil: phototoxicity from furanocoumarins (bergapten + xanthotoxin) — Cat.1 = 0%, Cat.2 = 0%, Cat.3 = 0%, Cat.4 = 0.85%, Cat.5A = 0.85%, Cat.5B = 0.85%; EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2271 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% (phototoxic-capped) in fine fragrance for green-vegetal / spicy-herbal / niche-modern accords; Cat.1 (lip) avoided; cold-pressed seed oil preferred over distilled (richer phthalide profile)

odor.description: Warm, sweet-spicy, rich cinnamon with honeyed-balsamic depth; dominated by cinnamaldehyde (65-80%) with eugenol + benzyl benzoate supporting; higher-quality bark oil distinct from harsher leaf oil

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cinnamomum verum bark oil: Cat.1 = 0.005%, Cat.2 = 0.002%, Cat.3 = 0.01%, Cat.4 = 0.05%, Cat.5A = 0.01%, Cat.5B = 0.007%, Cat.5C = 0.01%, Cat.5D = 0.002%, Cat.9 = 0.12%, Cat.10A-B = 0.12%, Cat.11A-B = 0.06%, Cat.12 Not Limited; tight caps driven by cinnamaldehyde (H317 strong sensitiser, EU 26 allergen) + eugenol; FEMA 2291 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.005-0.05% in fine fragrance for warm-spicy Oriental accords; tighter cap than clove bud; skin sensitiser disclosure required

odor.description: Soft, sweet-apple, hay-herbaceous with characteristic fruity-ester depth (isobutyl + amyl angelate-tiglate) and gentle herbal-medicinal undertone; <1% chamazulene (contrast to German); defining apple-hay accord

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Chamaemelum nobile flower oil: no specific concentration restriction on oil; EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene + linalool traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2272 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for apple-hay-herbaceous accords; English + French origins; distinct from German/blue chamomile chemically + visually

odor.description: Harsh, sharper, more medicinal-phenolic than bud oil; less sweet fruity-woody nuance; industrial eugenol source

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Syzygium aromaticum leaf oil: Cat.1 = 0.02%, Cat.2 = 0.005%, Cat.3 = 0.04%, Cat.4 = 0.14%, Cat.5A = 0.03%, Cat.5B = 0.02%, Cat.5C = 0.03%, Cat.5D = 0.005%, Cat.9 = 0.23%, Cat.10A-B = 0.23%, Cat.11A-B = 0.1%, Cat.12 Not Limited; tighter than bud oil because eugenol 80-88%; skin sensitiser H317; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.08% in fine fragrance; FEMA 2324 GRAS; primary industrial source of eugenol for isolation and synthesis of downstream materials (isoeugenol / vanillin route); rougher smell than bud oil — prefer bud oil for direct perfumery use

odor.description: Sweet, fruity-rummy-honey-balsamic with characteristic davanone-davana ether warm-amber-tobacco-orange undertone; uniquely 'cognac-vintage-port' profile; defining south Indian niche-luxury material; transforms remarkably on skin into fruity-floral 'persona drift' (each wearer's skin chemistry produces different note shift)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Artemisia pallens oil: no specific concentration restriction (low thujone vs. wormwood); EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2359 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-2% in fine fragrance for fruity-amber / niche-Indian / honey-tobacco accords; premium Indian export (~20,000 USD/kg davanone-rich grade); modern niche cornerstone (Carner Barcelona, Roja, Memo)

odor.description: Sharp, fresh, slightly bitter-citrus with characteristic 'grapefruit-zest' green-sulfurous undertone; defining 90% d-limonene + 0.1-1% nootkatone (signature) + 1-p-menthene-8-thiol (~ppb level — iconic grapefruit thiol); Florida pink/white grades; modern niche cornerstone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus paradisi oil: phototoxicity Cat.4 = 4.0%; FEMA 2530 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance for fresh-citrus / sport / modern-fougère accords

odor.description: Soft, smoky-balsamic, woody-rose with characteristic 'tea-rose-incense' rosy-smoky-balsamic undertone; defining guaiol + bulnesol sesquiterpenes; iconic Latin American + South-American luxury wood (Bulnesia sarmientoi); modern niche-luxury cornerstone (Hermès Eau de Gentiane Blanche, Diptyque Tam Dao)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Bulnesia sarmientoi oil: cumulative cap; CITES II (overharvest concern); FEMA 2534 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for guaiac / smoky-rosy-wood / luxury-niche accords

odor.description: Rich, dark, amber-animalic with deep leathery-balsamic complexity and pine-honey-ambergris undertone; dominated by labdane diterpenes (labdanoxide, viridiflorol, α-cadinol) + aromatic aldehydes; the defining natural of amber + oriental base accords

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cistus ladanifer extract (labdanum): no specific concentration restriction on full absolute; trace allergens (EU 26 — limonene / linalool); peroxide ≤10 mmol/L (viridiflorol autoxidation); FEMA 2552 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance for amber base accords; Spanish (Alvarez) + Moroccan origins; concrete → absolute → resinoid successive extraction grades; essential natural foundation of classical oriental + amber

odor.description: Strong, warm, savoury-celery-spicy with characteristic 'maggi-bouillon' meaty-soup signature; defining 30-70% ligustilide + sedanenolide + butylphthalide profile; iconic 'soup-bouillon' material — the molecule that 'flavours' Maggi seasoning + classic European stock; modern niche perfumery for savoury / vegetal / chypre-stock accords

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Levisticum officinale root oil: cumulative cap with celery + lovage phthalides; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; light + air sensitive (rapid ligustilide isomerization); FEMA 2650 GRAS; pregnancy contra-indicated (uterine stimulant)
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for savoury-vegetal / chypre-stock / niche-modern accords; defines 'maggi note' — classic supporting accord in mossy-chypre + culinary fragrances

odor.description: Fresh, resinous-terpenic with characteristic lemon-peppery top and incense-cedar-balsamic depth; α-pinene + limonene + α-thujene dominate top, octyl acetate + incensole modify heart; the sacred resin's distinctive meditation-church character

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Boswellia carterii oil: no specific concentration restriction (different Boswellia species have different limonene/caryophyllene profiles); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene 10-20%); FEMA 2816 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for resinous-fresh accords; Somali / Omani / Ethiopian origins; distillate cleaner than CO2 extract; complements labdanum + myrrh in classical incense base

odor.description: Warm, bitter-medicinal, aromatic-balsamic with characteristic dry-earthy mushroom-furanoid depth; furanoeudesma-1,3-diene + curzerene + lindestrene dominate; the sacred bitter counterpoint to frankincense's fresh resin

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Commiphora myrrha oil: no specific concentration restriction; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (sesquiterpene furan content — autoxidation); EU 26 allergen traces (limonene); FEMA 2766 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.2-2% in fine fragrance for balsamic-oriental base; Ethiopian + Somali origins; resinoid (solvent-extracted) darker + sweeter than distillate; irreplaceable for bitter-resinous accords

odor.description: Bright, sparkling, fresh-floral with citrus-green top and honeyed-orange blossom depth; complex linalool-dominated heart with methyl anthranilate indolic lift; the ultimate bridge between citrus and floral families

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus aurantium flower oil: no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + limonene + geraniol + farnesol); FEMA 2771 GRAS; not phototoxic (distillate rather than expressed); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for bright floral-citrus heart; cornerstone of classical Cologne (eau de Cologne); Tunisian + Moroccan origins; steam-distilled vs. petitgrain (leaf) and orange flower absolute (solvent)

odor.description: Musky-ambery with wine-like sweet-herbal character; complex, soft-spicy with hay-tobacco undertone; a signature anchor of fougère and chypre accords

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool 6-24%, geraniol 0.5-5%, linalyl acetate derivatives)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; workhorse of aromatic fougère and chypre; key to classical men's fragrance structures; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, detergent, candle, air care)

odor.description: Cleaner, more camphoraceous-eucalyptus than Dalmatian sage; lavender-sage hybrid with pine lift; thujone-free so safer for cosmetic use

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry for S. lavandulifolia; caps cascade from camphor (~15-25%) and 1,8-cineole (~20-40%) components: Cat.1 = 2%, Cat.2 = 0.56%, Cat.3 = 2.96%, Cat.4 = 20%, Cat.5A = 4.4%, Cat.5B = 3.04%, Cat.5D = 0.96% (conservative ceilings assuming 25% camphor — batch COA camphor % drives actual cap); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool traces)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance; preferred sage oil for leave-on cosmetics due to absence of thujone neurotoxicity risk; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, detergent, candle, air care)

odor.description: Warm, spicy-herbal, phenolic with green peppery undertone and balsamic body; carvacrol/thymol chemotype kin to oregano and thyme

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry; caps cascade from carvacrol (~35-50%) + thymol (~5-20%): Cat.1 = 0.2%, Cat.2 = 0.6%, Cat.3 = 2.4%, Cat.4 = 7.2%, Cat.5A = 1.6%, Cat.5B = 1.1%, Cat.5D = 0.36%, Cat.9 = 1.9%, Cat.10A = 7.2% (conservative ceilings assuming 45% phenol total — batch COA drives actual cap); EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool traces)
safety.usage: Typical 0.02-0.3% in fine fragrance; phenolic — avoid high leave-on; used in herbal functional perfumery and food flavouring under FEMA 3019

odor.description: Camphoraceous, cineole-rich, herbal-lavender with medicinal and harsher character; less sweet than true lavender

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cat.12 = 100%; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; contains 25-40% 1,8-cineole (not an EU allergen but may irritate sensitive skin)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance; common in fougère, aromatic, herbal accords and functional perfumery

odor.description: Sweet, soft, fresh-citrus with characteristic 'sweet-tangerine-peel' juicy-fruity undertone; very similar to mandarin (same species) but slightly redder/sweeter — Citrus reticulata var. tangerina (Florida/American grade); ~80% d-limonene

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus reticulata oil: phototoxicity Cat.4 = 4.0%; FEMA 3041 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 1-8% in fine fragrance — soft-citrus alternative to mandarin

odor.description: Sharp, herbaceous, camphoraceous-bitter with strong thujone-driven menthol-cool bite and faint floral undertone; defining Tanacetum vulgare profile — typically 60-95% β-thujone (chemotype-dependent: also α-thujone, camphor, umbellulone variants); historical use in vermouth, perfumery (now restricted) and traditional medicine; Asteraceae cluster sister to Artemisia genus (wormwood, mugwort)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — thujone-rich (60-95%) chemotype neurotoxicity / abortifacient concern; severely restricted; banned in EU food (Reg 1334/2008); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; modern use limited to historical / industrial / aromatherapy at controlled doses
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.1% in fine fragrance for Asteraceae herbal accords (wormwood/mugwort/tansy chord); historical absinthe/vermouth flavour replaced by thujone-free chemotypes; aromatherapy + traditional medicine; commercial use minimal due to thujone restrictions

odor.description: Warm, sweet-herbaceous, anise-licorice with green-fennel undertone and faint camphoraceous lift; characteristic estragole-dominated French chemotype (Russian chemotype higher in trans-anethole)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Artemisia dracunculus (tarragon) oil: Cat.1 = 0.01%, Cat.2 = 0.01%, Cat.3 = 0.06%, Cat.4 = 0.17%, Cat.5A = 0.05%, Cat.5B = 0.03%, Cat.5C = 0.05%, Cat.5D = 0.01%, Cat.9 = 0.32%, Cat.10A = 0.32%, Cat.11A-B = 0.15%, Cat.12 Not Limited; estragole-driven restriction (60-80% methyl chavicol, CMR 2 genotoxic carcinogen); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.005-0.05% in fine fragrance; trace marker for herbal / Fougère / anise accords; prefer estragole-free fractions or synthetic reconstitutions at higher levels; FEMA 3042 GRAS for flavour within botanical-extract context (EU 1334/2008 estragole limits apply)

odor.description: Deep, earthy, smoky-woody with distinct balsamic-leather depth and faint sweet-rooty warmth; complex sesquiterpenoid profile dominated by khusimol + isovalencenol + α-vetivone; Haitian origin considered benchmark

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction on Chrysopogon zizanioides oil; EU 26 allergen labelling generally not required; FEMA 3103 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (sesquiterpene autoxidation)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance for Chypre / Oriental / masculine base; cornerstone alongside patchouli / sandalwood / oakmoss; Javanese origin sweeter; Bourbon / Réunion is rarer premium grade

odor.description: Crisp, sweet, resinous-needle with characteristic bornyl acetate-dominated (30-40%) coniferous heart and camphoraceous-forest top; softer and sweeter than Scots pine; the signature Christmas tree fir note

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Abies sibirica leaf oil: follows pine-family caps (Cat.4 ≈ 10%); peroxide ≤10 mmol/L (α-pinene/camphene autoxidation); EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene traces); skin sensitiser H317 on oxidised material
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for sweet coniferous accords; preferred over Scots pine where softer character wanted; classical ingredient of forest / cologne fougère

odor.description: Herbaceous-camphoraceous, standard-Provence lavandin profile — rounded balance of linalyl acetate (~30-35%), linalool (~28-34%), and camphor (~6-8%); generic commercial blend typical of Provence distillers; between Grosso and Abrial in camphor intensity

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry for L. × intermedia; caps cascade from camphor (~6-8%) content and linalool + linalyl acetate allergen fraction; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + limonene traces)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; generic Provence grade — functional perfumery workhorse (soap, detergent, candle, air care); FEMA 2618 GRAS same as cultivar-specific lavandin grades

odor.description: Strong, camphoraceous, herbal-peppery with minty-sharp top and warm culinary-sage depth; thujone-rich, true culinary sage character

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cat.1 = 0.1%, Cat.2 = 0.04%, Cat.3 = 0.2%, Cat.4 = 0.8%, Cat.5A = 0.3%, Cat.5B = 0.2%, Cat.5D = 0.06%; thujone-driven neurotoxicity ceiling; EU 26 allergen labelling
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance; restricted in leave-on cosmetics due to α-thujone 15-50% content; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, detergent, candle, air care)

odor.description: Sharp, herbaceous-camphoraceous, slightly bitter with characteristic chamazulene-driven sweet-blue depth and faint floral-balsamic undertone; defining Achillea millefolium profile — chamazulene 5-25% (formed during steam distillation from matricin precursor) gives the iconic deep-blue colour; sabinene + 1,8-cineole + camphor + α-pinene round out the camphoraceous bite; Asteraceae cluster sister to Chamomile (also chamazulene-rich)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — yarrow: caps cascade from sabinene + camphor + thujone (low %) traces; some chemotypes contain β-thujone (avoid pregnancy); EU 26 allergen (limonene, linalool traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.3% in fine fragrance for camphoraceous-floral-blue accords; aromatherapy + traditional medicine (anti-inflammatory); pairs with Chamomile German for blue-azulene effects; ultra-premium 'Blue Yarrow' grade with high chamazulene content

odor.description: Rich, warm, heady floral with honey-fruity depth and indolic animalic undertone; Jasminum grandiflorum classic with benzyl acetate brightness over indole narcotic warmth

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Jasminum grandiflorum absolute: Cat.1 = 0.7%, Cat.2 = 0.16%, Cat.3 = 1.1%, Cat.4 = 4%, Cat.5A = 1%, Cat.5B = 0.5%, Cat.5C = 1%, Cat.5D = 0.16%, Cat.9 = 2.2%, Cat.10A-B = 2.2%, Cat.11A-B = 1.1%, Cat.12 Not Limited; driven by benzyl benzoate + benzyl salicylate allergen load; EU 26 allergen labelling (benzyl acetate + benzyl benzoate + benzyl alcohol + farnesol + geraniol + linalool); FEMA 2598 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-2% in fine fragrance; cornerstone Floral / Oriental heart; Grasse + Egyptian origins preferred; pairs with rose / ylang / sandalwood / tuberose

odor.description: Deep, hay-floral, intensely indolic-animalic with characteristic 'narcotic-narcissus' green-leathery undertone and subtle hyacinth/jasmine cross; defining benzyl benzoate + cinnamate + indole + paracresol; ultra-premium classical floral (~$5000-8000/kg) — rare French Auvergne harvest

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Narcissus poeticus absolute: cumulative cap with floral absolutes; EU 26 allergen labelling (benzyl benzoate + benzyl alcohol + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; very expensive
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for narcissus / hay-floral / classical-luxury accords

odor.description: Sweet, warm, honey-violet with characteristic farnesol-floral honeyed-balsamic depth and subtle anisic-balsamic-leathery undertone; warmer + more honeyed-spicy than mimosa absolute; defining 'sweet acacia' floral; classical luxury material — Frédéric Malle Une Fleur de Cassie, Caron Tabac Blond

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Acacia farnesiana absolute: cumulative cap with floral absolutes; EU 26 allergen labelling (benzyl alcohol + farnesol + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2235 GRAS (food-flavour use); premium price (~$3000-5000/kg)
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for cassie-honey-acacia / leathery-floral / classical-luxury accords

odor.description: Rich, warm, leathery-animalic with characteristic 'birch-tar-vanillin-tobacco' deep-balsamic depth and subtle musky undertone; modern synthetic replacement for natural castoreum (beaver gland secretion — animal-cruelty-banned in most markets); defining accord = phenolic + tar + macrocyclic musk + benzoin

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — castoreum substitute: cumulative cap with phenolic/tar components; FEMA 2261 GRAS (vanilla/leather flavour); cruelty-free synthetic blend (vs. natural Castor canadensis)
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for leather / animalic-warm / chypre-leather / classical-cuir accords

odor.description: Warmer, sweeter, more resinous-balsamic than Virginian; atlantones add honeyed-animalic depth; richer character for fine fragrance use

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cedrus atlantica wood oil: no specific concentration restriction; FEMA 2263 GRAS; CITES Appendix III listed (Morocco) — sustainable sourcing required
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; preferred for fine fragrance where richer balsamic-animalic cedar character wanted

odor.description: Fresh, lemony-resinous with characteristic 'pine-incense' citrus-balsamic top and warm-resin base; defining 30-50% limonene + 20-30% elemicin + 10-15% elemol; iconic Filipino resin (cleaner-fresher vs. frankincense)

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-12 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Canarium luzonicum oil: cumulative cap with elemicin (genotoxicity concerns at high dose); FEMA 2407 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for elemi / resin-citrus / lemon-incense accords

odor.description: Intensely green, bitter, resinous-leafy with characteristic pyrazine crushed-leaf top (2-methoxy-3-isobutylpyrazine signature) and balsamic-ambrette depth; the sharpest natural green — defining Chanel No.19 / Vent Vert signature

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Ferula galbaniflua oil: no specific concentration restriction; peroxide ≤10 mmol/L (monoterpene + pyrazine content); EU 26 allergen labelling (limonene traces); FEMA 2501 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance; Iranian origin; use sparingly — extremely dominant; classical ingredient of Chypre + Green-Floral accords

odor.description: Fresh, herbaceous, slightly camphoraceous-fruity with characteristic woody-resinous chamomile-yarrow undertone; distinct from Roman + German chamomile — sharper, less fruity-rummy, more resinous; commercial 'Moroccan Blue Chamomile' (despite no chamazulene) — Ormenis multicaulis (= Cladanthus mixtus) chemotype rich in santolina alcohol + α-pinene + camphor

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Ormenis multicaulis oil: no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (α-pinene oxidation); FEMA n/a
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for herbal-camphor / chamomile-yarrow / Mediterranean accords; cheaper alternative to true chamomiles for functional fragrance

odor.description: Soft, fresh, lilac-flower with characteristic 'spring-bloom' green-floral powdery undertone; synthetic accord (Syringa natural extract impossible — flower too low-yielding); built from terpineol + hydroxycitronellal + benzyl alcohol + indole + heliotropin; iconic spring floral material

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — lilac accord components: cumulative cap with each constituent; commercial supply via specialty captives (Givaudan/Firmenich/Symrise)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for lilac / spring-floral / classical-bouquet accords

odor.description: Heavy, narcotic, white-floral with creamy-lactonic depth and green-rubbery headspace facet; benzyl benzoate + methyl benzoate + methyl salicylate + methyl anthranilate + indole drive the complex profile; unique living-flower vs. absolute divergence due to enfleurage-only methyl anthranilate retention

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Polianthes tuberosa flower absolute: no specific concentration restriction on full absolute (benzyl salicylate + benzyl benzoate drive allergen caps); EU 26 + 2023/1545 allergen labelling (benzyl salicylate + benzyl benzoate + benzyl alcohol + methyl 2-octynoate traces); FEMA 3099 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance (extremely expensive — Indian Grasse origins); hexane extract (concrete → absolute); reconstituted via benzyl salicylate / methyl benzoate / indole base

odor.description: Rich, warm, sweet-balsamic with deep creamy-vanillic body and faint boozy-rum lift; complex beyond synthetic vanillin — includes guaiacol / anisyl / piperonal trace complexity; Bourbon Madagascar is the reference standard

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction on vanilla absolute; vanillin moiety (1.5-3% of absolute) carries EU 26 / 2023/1545 allergen labelling; FEMA 3105 GRAS; solvent-extracted from cured Vanilla planifolia pods
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for gourmand / Oriental / Chypre base; cornerstone of Shalimar / Angel / classic vanillic compositions; blends with tonka / benzoin / labdanum / tobacco; Bourbon (Madagascar) / Mexican / Tahitian origins — Tahitian has higher piperonal content

odor.description: Intensely green, cucumber-leafy with characteristic fatty-watery undertone and metallic-chlorophyll depth; dominated by 2,6-nonadienal + 2,4-heptadienal with cis-3-hexenol; defines ultra-green floral-leaf accord

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Viola odorata leaf absolute: no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen traces; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (dienal content — autoxidation)
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance; Egyptian + French origins; expensive — solvent extract; classical green-floral accent (Balenciaga Paris, Fahrenheit)

odor.description: Sweet, juicy, ripe-mango with characteristic 'tropical-mango-flesh' creamy-sulfurous undertone; defining synthetic mango = δ-decalactone + furfuryl thiol traces + ionone-class + ester base; modern tropical accord

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — mango accord: cumulative cap with constituents; commercial supply via specialty captives
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance for mango / tropical-fruit / Asian-niche accords

odor.description: Soft, powdery-floral, warm-anisic-honey with characteristic 'fluffy-yellow-pompom' airy fluffy-balsamic depth and subtle violet-cucumber undertone; defining heliotropin + anisaldehyde + palmitic acid + linear hydrocarbons profile; iconic French Côte d'Azur winter perfumery material; 'eternal mimosa' classics — Caron Farnesiana, Hermès Champs-Elysées, Frédéric Malle Une Fleur de Cassie

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Acacia dealbata absolute: cumulative cap with floral absolutes; EU 26 allergen labelling (benzyl alcohol + linalool + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; expensive (~$2500/kg) — most modern fragrance uses synthetic mimosa accord
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for mimosa-yellow-pompom / powdery-anisic-floral / classical-winter-floral accords

odor.description: Sweet, honeyed-floral, summer-bloom with characteristic 'tropical-honeysuckle' jasmine-rose-vanilla undertone; synthetic accord (Lonicera natural extract impossible); built from benzyl acetate + linalool + ionones + indole + vanillin

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — honeysuckle accord: cumulative cap with constituents
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance for honeysuckle / sweet-floral / summer-bouquet accords

odor.description: Sweet, powdery, almond-vanilla-cherry with characteristic 'cherry-pie-flower' marzipan-floral undertone; defining synthetic heliotrope = heliotropin (piperonal) + benzaldehyde + vanillin + benzyl alcohol; iconic Soft Floral / Floral Amber bridge material

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — heliotrope accord: cumulative cap with constituents
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance for heliotrope / cherry-pie / classical-powdery-floral accords

odor.description: Lighter, drier, more balsamic-resinous than Mysore; less creamy-milky; santalol content 15-25% α + 10-15% β; sustainably cultivated alternative

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Santalum spicatum oil: caps approximately parallel to S. album (Cat.4 ≈ 9%); lighter santalol profile means subtler character at equivalent dosage; FEMA 3005 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 1-5% in fine fragrance; CITES-compliant plantation-grown (Western Australia since 1990s); preferred sustainable option for modern briefs

odor.description: Warm, spicy-earthy with characteristic 'fresh-turmeric-rhizome' woody-curry-camphoraceous undertone; defining ar-turmerone + α-zingiberene; iconic Indian Ayurvedic cuisine spice; modern niche-spice perfumery

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 6-10 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Curcuma longa oil: cumulative cap with spicy oils; FEMA 3085 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-0.5% in fine fragrance for turmeric / curry / niche-Indian spicy-warm accords

odor.description: Sweet, juicy, bright-orange with characteristic candy-orange undertone; defining 90-95% d-limonene + minor myrcene + linalool + decanal; iconic functional perfumery cornerstone; Brazilian/Florida cold-pressed grade dominant

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus sinensis oil: low phototoxicity (lower furanocoumarin than lemon/lime); FEMA 2825 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; sustainability-positive (citrus juicing co-product)
safety.usage: Typical 1-15% in fine fragrance + heavy use in functional perfumery (cleaning/laundry)

odor.description: Rich, honeyed, warm-floral with deep indolic-animalic undertone and waxy-sweet orange blossom character; deeper and more narcotic than steam-distilled neroli; methyl anthranilate + indole prominent

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus aurantium flower absolute: caps approximately parallel to neroli oil; higher indole + methyl anthranilate concentration than distillate; EU 26 allergen labelling; FEMA 2772 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for deep heart-base floral; complements neroli oil with animalic-indolic depth

odor.description: Deep, rich, honeyed-smoky with warm tobacco-leaf character, hay-coumarinic undertone, and distinct tea-dried fruit depth; cured Nicotiana tabacum leaf extracted after fermentation; essentially nicotine-free (<50 ppm) in properly processed absolute

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone cap on nicotine-free tobacco absolute; EU Tobacco Products Directive does not apply to perfumery use at <0.1% levels; FEMA 3092 GRAS (flavour); specifically demand nicotine-free grade from supplier (CoA required for <50 ppm)
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for rich-dry Oriental / Chypre / Fougère base; pairs with labdanum / tonka / oakmoss / leather accords; quality varies dramatically by cure method (flue-cured vs air-cured vs fire-cured); pioneering tobacco fragrance: Guerlain Habit Rouge (1965)

odor.description: Rich, sweet, cinnamic-balsamic with characteristic 'incense-warm' resinous-leather undertone; defining cinnamic acid + cinnamate esters + styrene traces; classical Greek/Anatolian balsam (Levant) + American sweetgum (Liquidambar)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Liquidambar styraciflua resinoid: cumulative cap with cinnamic balsams; EU 26 allergen labelling (cinnamic compounds); FEMA 3036 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for resinous-balsamic / incense / classical-amber accords

odor.description: Rich, warm, sweet coumarinic-vanilla with almond-caramel depth and distinct hay-tobacco lift; anchored by 1-3% coumarin plus trace phenylpropanoids; classic gourmand-Oriental heart-base material

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Dipteryx odorata bean absolute: caps driven by coumarin content (1-3%); calculate oil dose to keep coumarin below Cat.4 = 1.6% ceiling; EU 26 allergen labelling (coumarin moiety); FEMA 3086 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for gourmand-Oriental base; cornerstone of Fougère (with lavender + oakmoss) and modern gourmand compositions (e.g. Tobacco Vanille); coumarin allergen disclosure required

odor.description: Warm, spicy-clove with sharp camphoraceous-medicinal lift and soft herbaceous-sweet body; distinctly heavier and more phenolic than sweet basil; eugenol (30-60%) + methyl eugenol (10-30%) drive the clove-warm character, β-caryophyllene (10-30%) adds woody-peppery depth, 1,8-cineole (~5%) contributes camphoraceous lift; defining note of Ayurvedic / sacred-incense / naturalistic Indian compositions

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry for O. tenuiflorum; caps cascade from methyl eugenol (IARC 2B possibly carcinogenic to humans, 10-30% in Tulsi) and eugenol (30-60%, Schedule B sensitiser) — Standard withdraws methyl eugenol from leave-on cosmetic categories: Cat.1 ≤ 0.0004%, Cat.2 ≤ 0.0003%, Cat.3 ≤ 0.001%, Cat.4 ≤ 0.0015%, Cat.5A-D leave-on ≤ 0.0001%; EU 1334/2008 restricts methyl eugenol in flavouring (ML varies by food category); EU 26 allergen labelling (eugenol); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical <0.01% in fine fragrance for trace naturalistic marker use; NOT for leave-on cosmetic formulations where methyl eugenol exposure would exceed IFRA caps; suited for incense, room spray, niche ambergris/oriental/Indian-accord work; widely used in Ayurvedic aromatherapy outside regulated cosmetic use; no FEMA — culinary use via whole-leaf herb, not essential-oil form

odor.description: Rich, deep, sweet-herbaceous with warm anisic-green body, tobacco-hay undertone and pronounced long-lasting tenacity; concentrated solvent extract of Ocimum basilicum leaves — retains volatile character of the oil while adding heavier non-volatile notes (chlorophyll, waxes, resinous fraction) that make it a base-note modifier rather than a top-note oil

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone restriction for O. basilicum absolute; caps cascade from the same volatile compounds as Sweet Basil Oil — methyl chavicol + methyl eugenol content drives tight leave-on limits (European/linalool CT absolute permits higher leave-on than Exotic CT absolute); EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool, eugenol, limonene traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; store cool/dark — absolute is more prone to oxidation than the oil due to chlorophyll content
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.3% in fine fragrance as a herbal-fixative modifier; fougère / chypre / naturalistic compositions where sweet basil character is wanted with heavier body than the oil provides; functional perfumery use minimal — cost and colour (dark green) limit applications; not a standalone food flavouring material

odor.description: Sharp, warm, clove-phenolic with thyme-medicinal body and dry herbaceous tail; distinct from Sweet / Holy Basil — O. gratissimum chemotypes centre on eugenol (40-70%) or thymol (in Nigerian thymol CT, 30-50%); intensely clove-like character in eugenol CT, phenolic-thymic in thymol CT; used in West African / pan-African naturalistic work and traditional antiseptic / insect-repellent preparations

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone oil-level entry for O. gratissimum; caps cascade from dominant phenol (eugenol or thymol depending on CT, 40-70% combined) + trace methyl eugenol: eugenol CT gated by IFRA eugenol caps (Cat.5A ≤ 0.5%); thymol CT gated by IFRA thymol caps (Cat.5A ≤ 0.52%, tighter than eugenol); EU 26 allergen labelling (eugenol mandatory); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical <0.1% in fine fragrance; chemotype selection critical — verify COA for eugenol vs thymol ratio; functional perfumery use moderate (soap, insect-repellent accords, traditional African medicinal products); no FEMA — not a standalone food-flavouring oil in major regulatory frameworks

odor.description: Warm, dry, soft-woody-peppery with subtle clove-spice undertone and faint balsamic tail; very low odor threshold and diffusive; sesquiterpene (C15) skeleton differentiates it from the C10 monoterpenes dominant elsewhere in basil / rosemary / thyme; defining sesquiterpene of Black Pepper (15-25%), Clove bud (5-15%), Cannabis, Hops, Holy Basil (10-30%), Rosemary / Clary Sage (2-8%)

performance.tenacity: Medium to High — 5-8 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone concentration restriction for β-caryophyllene; FEMA 2252 GRAS; GHS H304 aspiration hazard, H317 skin sensitiser (via oxidation products); peroxide ≤10 mmol/L and store cool/dark under inert gas — β-caryophyllene oxide (autoxidation product) is a known sensitiser; hydroperoxide formation is slower than monoterpene equivalents but still QC-relevant
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-2% in fine fragrance; woody-peppery modifier in pepper / clove / tobacco / cannabis-accord / oriental compositions; sesquiterpene body-builder — used where deeper warm-woody tenacity is wanted without adding phenolic or camphoraceous character; food flavouring under FEMA 2252 (spice / beverage); present as background sesquiterpene in most warm-woody naturals

odor.description: Soft, dry, woody-rooty with characteristic earthy-balsamic depth; sesquiterpene diol; defining 5-25% volatile of carrot seed oil; complementary to carotol for full Daucus chemotype; chemotaxonomic identifier

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — daucol: no specific concentration restriction; not on EU 26 allergen list; chemotaxonomic Daucus marker; very rare as standalone ingredient
safety.usage: Typical trace 0.01-0.2% in fine fragrance — usually delivered as part of carrot seed oil

safety.ifra: Awaiting curation — treat as restricted. Consult IFRA 51 + CosIng directly before use. (Auto-generated stub from PubChem.)
safety.usage: No curated usage range — start <0.1% in product and review against IFRA 51 / RIFM safety files before scaling.

odor.description: Soft, mint-rose-floral with characteristic 'minty-rosy-citronella' fresh-cooling undertone; pulegone reduction product; minor (~1-3%) Mentha species marker; chemotaxonomic + biosynthetic intermediate (pulegone → iso-pulegol → menthone → menthol cascade)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — iso-pulegol: cumulative cap with pulegone-class; FEMA 2962 GRAS at trace; pregnancy contra-indicated (pulegone-related)
safety.usage: Typical trace 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance — chemotaxonomic + bridging molecule for mint/rose accords

odor.description: Sharp, herbaceous, peppermint-camphoraceous with cooling ketonic mint character and faint medicinal undertone; classic chemotype marker for Eucalyptus dives (~40-55%) and a minor constituent of Japanese mint (Mentha arvensis) — historically the industrial precursor for synthetic menthol via piperitone → menthone → menthol hydrogenation route

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — piperitone: not on EU 26 allergen list; FEMA 2910 GRAS at low ppm; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.2% in fine fragrance for peppermint-eucalyptus / herbal-cooling accords; major industrial use is feedstock for synthetic menthol manufacture (piperitone → menthone → menthol catalytic route, classical Takasago process)

odor.description: Powerful, sharp, minty-camphoraceous with characteristic 'hot-pulegone' burnt-mint pungent character; defining 60-90% volatile of pennyroyal oil; iconic but HEPATOTOXIC monoterpene ketone; metabolizes to menthofuran which forms reactive intermediates causing liver failure; multiple historical fatalities at >2g acute dose

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 3-5 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — pulegone: cumulative cap with pennyroyal; FEMA 2963 GRAS at trace; EU restricted to <0.1% in oral care + 0.02% leave-on cosmetics; metabolic hepatotoxicity via menthofuran intermediate; pregnancy strictly contra-indicated
safety.usage: Typical trace 0.001-0.05% — heavily restricted; usually delivered as part of natural pennyroyal/peppermint oil rather than isolate; mostly flavour use only

odor.description: Very powerful, sharp, dry-medicinal, phenolic-warm with cool camphoraceous bite and herbal-spicy undertone; defining thyme / oregano phenol

performance.tenacity: High — 8-12 hours on strip (crystalline, low vapour pressure)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 restriction — Cat.1 = 0.07%, Cat.2 = 0.2%, Cat.3 = 0.8%, Cat.4 = 2.4%, Cat.5A = 0.52%, Cat.5B = 0.36%, Cat.5C = 0.72%, Cat.5D = 0.11%, Cat.9 = 0.62%, Cat.10A = 2.4%, Cat.10B = 2.4%, Cat.11A = 0.08%, Cat.11B = 0.17%, Cat.12 = not restricted; dermal sensitiser and irritant to skin/eyes (GHS H302 H314 H411); FEMA 3066 GRAS as flavouring at low ppm (mouthwash, toothpaste, food)
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.1% in fine fragrance; 0.01-0.1% in oral care (toothpaste, mouthwash) as antiseptic flavouring; avoid high leave-on cosmetic doses — IFRA caps enforced; widely used in functional perfumery (soap, detergent) and dental/oral flavouring

odor.description: Fresh, sweet, citrus with clean linalyl-acetate character; slightly less nuance than expressed oil (coumarinic top-note fraction stripped during FCF processing) but phototoxic-safe

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Bergamot FCF/BF (furanocoumarin reduced, bergapten ≤20 ppm in oil): no phototoxic restriction in finished product; Cat.1-12 not limited by the furanocoumarin standard; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + limonene) still applies
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance and functional perfumery; preferred over expressed bergamot oil for leave-on cosmetics, sunscreen, face creams, and any formulation with UV-exposure scenarios

odor.description: Warm, smoky-phenolic, medicinal with characteristic whiskey-barrel-smoke signature; natural in smoke flavoring, coffee, whiskey, wood creosote; cornerstone of smoky / tar / leather reconstitution

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — guaiacol: no specific concentration restriction on isolate; natural phenolic — sensitiser concern at high levels (H317); FEMA 2532 GRAS (massive smoke / whiskey flavour use)
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.2% in fine fragrance; trace in smoky / leather / tobacco / whisky accords; pair with p-cresol + methyl eugenol for richer smoky profile

odor.description: Sweet, balsamic-vanillic with characteristic 'almond-vanilla-cinnamic' powdery-warm undertone; defining benzoic acid + vanillin + cinnamic acid esters; iconic Amber base fixative; Siam grade preferred (sweeter, more vanillic)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Styrax benzoin resinoid: cumulative cap with balsamic resins; FEMA 2133 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for amber / balsamic / fixative accords

odor.description: Deep, dry-resinous, balsamic-green with characteristic resin-coumarin-balsamic depth and subtle bitter-pine undertone; tenacious base note version of galbanum (vs. fresher oil); used as fixative for green-galbanum chypre accord

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Ferula gummosa resinoid: cumulative cap with galbanum oil + absolute; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (α-pinene oxidation); EU 26 allergen labelling traces; FEMA 2501 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-1% in fine fragrance for green-chypre / fixative / classical-resin accords; foundational for Vent Vert (Balmain) + Chanel No.19 type

odor.description: Deep, earthy, forest-floor with green-leathery depth and distinct mossy-woody complexity; anchored by methyl β-orcinol carboxylate (evernyl) + atranol/chloroatranol; essential Chypre foundation

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Evernia prunastri extract: Cat.1 = 0.05%, Cat.2 = 0.01%, Cat.3 = 0.08%, Cat.4 = 0.3%, Cat.5A = 0.07%, Cat.5B = 0.04%, Cat.5C = 0.07%, Cat.5D = 0.01%, Cat.9 = 0.4%, Cat.10A-B = 0.4%, Cat.11A-B = 0.2%, Cat.12 Not Limited; extensively restricted after SCCNFP 2000 opinion — atranol + chloroatranol limited to <100 ppm (often <2 ppm commercial); EU 26 allergen labelling; FEMA 2795 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.2% in fine fragrance — tight allergen caps have reshaped Chypre construction since 2000s; IFRA 51 oakmoss grades are atranol-stripped; classic unrestricted grade retained for niche/vintage restoration

odor.description: Rich, sweet, cinnamic-vanillic-floral with characteristic 'church-incense-balsam' warm-spicy depth; defining cinnamate + benzoate esters; classical Latin-American balsam (Hermès Eau d'Hermès, Guerlain Habit Rouge)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Myroxylon balsamum tolu: cumulative cap with balsamics; EU 26 allergen labelling (cinnamic alcohol/aldehyde); skin sensitizer; FEMA 3070 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for cinnamic-balsamic / classical-amber accords

odor.description: Rich, sweet, vanillic-balsamic with deep cinnamic-resinous warmth; dominated by coniferyl benzoate (60-80% Siam) + vanillin (0.5-1%) + benzyl benzoate; irreplaceable sweet amber fixative

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Styrax tonkinensis / benzoin extract: Cat.1-2 = 0.3-0.6%, Cat.3 = 1.0%, Cat.4 = 6.0%, Cat.5A = 2.0%, Cat.5B = 1.0%, Cat.5C = 2.0%, Cat.5D = 0.3%, Cat.9-12 Not Limited; IFRA sensitiser restriction (H317 — coniferyl benzoate); EU 26 + 2023/1545 allergen labelling; FEMA 2133 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 1-5% in fine fragrance for sweet balsamic base; Siam benzoin (Laos) sweeter and simpler than Sumatra benzoin (more cinnamic + smoky); key vanilla-amber fixative

odor.description: Rich, sweet, balsamic-warm with characteristic 'sweet-myrrh-vanillic' resinous-amber depth and subtle anise-licorice undertone; defining bisabolene-class sesquiterpenes + furanoeudesmanes; iconic classical balsamic (Coty L'Origan, Caron Shocking)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Commiphora guidotti resinoid: cumulative cap with myrrh-class; FEMA 2776 GRAS; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for opoponax / sweet-balsamic / classical-amber accords

odor.description: Fresh, green-anisic with characteristic 'fresh-dill-frond' α-phellandrene + (Z)-dill ether (anethofuran) profile; cleaner + less spicy vs. seed oil; cucumber-celery-fennel undertone; defining culinary fresh-dill aromatic

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 3-6 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Anethum graveolens weed oil: cumulative cap with phellandrenes + apiole-class; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2383 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for fresh-green / culinary-herb / niche-natural accords

odor.description: Drier, more leathery-balsamic than oakmoss; spruce/fir host tree imparts coniferous-resinous undertone; lower chloroatranol content

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Pseudevernia furfuracea extract: Cat.1 = 0.05%, Cat.2 = 0.01%, Cat.3 = 0.1%, Cat.4 = 0.4%, Cat.5A = 0.1%, Cat.5B = 0.05%, Cat.5C = 0.1%, Cat.5D = 0.015%, Cat.9 = 0.5%, Cat.10A-B = 0.5%, Cat.11A-B = 0.25%, Cat.12 Not Limited; atranol/chloroatranol limit same as oakmoss; EU 26 allergen labelling; FEMA 3746 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.02-0.3% in fine fragrance; preferred over oakmoss for drier/cleaner mossy accord; often combined with oakmoss for rounded Chypre base

odor.description: Rich, deep, honey-curry-fenugreek with characteristic 'maple-syrup-fenugreek' herbaceous-balsamic undertone and subtle camphoraceous-amber depth; defining 4-methoxy-2,6,6-trimethylcyclohexa-1,3-diene + neryl acetate + sotolon traces; iconic Floral Amber cornerstone (Sublime Body Care, L'Artisan Parfumeur Mure et Musc)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Helichrysum italicum absolute: cumulative cap with floral absolutes; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; ultra-premium niche
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for immortelle / Floral Amber / curry-honey-fougère niche accords

odor.description: Fresher, greener, less-minty than Bourbon with lighter citronellol profile and sharper rose-leafy top; lower cost + different aromatic balance; functional fragrance workhorse

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — as per Bourbon cap (same species, different origin); EU 26 allergen labelling (citronellol + geraniol + linalool); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA 2508 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; Egyptian primary origin with lower citronellol / higher geraniol ratio than Bourbon

odor.description: Bright, sparkling, rosy-peppery with sweet berry-citrus lift; softer and fruitier than black pepper; α-phellandrene-dominated character distinct from Piper nigrum

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Schinus molle fruit oil: no standalone cap; α-phellandrene 40-60% requires BHT/tocopherol stabilisation (oxidised α-phellandrene is sensitising); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-0.5% in fine fragrance for modern sparkling-spicy accords; widely used in contemporary fragrance briefs (Jo Malone, Miller Harris, niche); distinct from Piper nigrum

odor.description: Softer, greener, more delicate than damascena; lighter honey-floral with pronounced green-waxy freshness and reduced spicy depth; preferred for transparent floral compositions

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Rosa centifolia flower oil: caps approximately parallel to Rosa damascena (Cat.4 ≈ 9%, Cat.5A ≈ 2.2%); allergen profile slightly lighter — lower eugenol + methyleugenol than damascena
safety.usage: Typical 0.2-3% in fine fragrance; valued in classical French perfumery ('rose de mai' tradition); smaller world production than damascena

odor.description: Dry, woody-vetiver-class with characteristic 'aged-papyrus-paper' earthy-rooty-smoky undertone; defining cyperone + α-cyperone sesquiterpenes (related to vetiver chemistry); iconic Egyptian/Arabic luxury cologne material (Memo Inle, Perris Monte Carlo)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cyperus papyrus oil: cumulative cap with sesquiterpenoid woods; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; modern niche supply
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for papyrus / Egyptian-niche / dry-paper-wood accords

odor.description: Soft, dry, woody-leathery with characteristic 'aged-teak-furniture' tannin-leather-balsamic undertone and subtle smoky depth; iconic Asian luxury wood; modern niche-Asian perfumery + furniture-fragrance (Le Labo Patchouli 24, Carner Barcelona)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Tectona grandis oil: cumulative cap; CITES-considered (Burmese teak overharvest); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance for teak / Asian-luxury-wood / leather-niche accords
