odor.description: Sweet, warm, hay-like with vanilla-almond nuance and distinct new-mown-hay / freshly-cut-grass character; anchors the classical Fougère accord; trigger for hay / tobacco / tonka impressions

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cat.1 = 0.3%, Cat.2 = 0.07%, Cat.3 = 0.46%, Cat.4 = 1.6%, Cat.5A = 0.4%, Cat.5B = 0.22%, Cat.5C = 0.4%, Cat.5D = 0.07%, Cat.9 = 0.88%, Cat.10A-B = 0.88%, Cat.11A-B = 0.44%, Cat.12 Not Limited; QRA2 skin sensitiser H317; EU 26 allergen labelling; EFSA TDI 0.1 mg/kg bw (hepatotoxic at high oral doses); FEMA 2356 GRAS at trace levels only
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; cornerstone of Fougère (Fougère Royale 1882) and Oriental compositions; natural component of tonka beans (1-3%), sweet clover, hay, sweetgrass, lavender (trace); allergen disclosure required >10 ppm leave-on / >100 ppm rinse-off

odor.description: Deep, rich, dried-hay-coumarin with characteristic 'meadow-stable-tobacco' grass-balsamic depth and subtle vanillic-honeyed undertone; defining 'foin coupe' (cut hay) accord; complex multi-source absolute (typically Hierochloe odorata + Melilotus + Anthoxanthum); foundational classical perfumery base note (Caron Pour Un Homme, Guerlain Mouchoir de Monsieur, Yves Saint Laurent Pour Homme)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — hay absolute: cumulative cap with coumarin; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (coumarin disclosure); some chemotypes contain dicoumarol traces (anticoagulant — moldy hay)
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for hay-coumarin / classical-fougère / barbershop / leathery-tobacco accords

odor.description: Herbaceous-camphoraceous, fresh-lavender with sharper medicinal-pine bite and faint floral undertone; chemotype-dependent — Grosso (camphor ~8%, linalyl acetate ~32%) is the common commercial grade; Abrial (camphor ~5%) is older and softer; Super (camphor ~3%, linalyl acetate ~40%) is the closest-to-true-lavender cultivar; Sumian is a niche mid-range chemotype

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cat.12 = 100%; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + linalyl acetate)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-5% in fine fragrance; cultivar selection drives application — Lavandin Super can substitute true lavender in cost-sensitive formulas; Grosso / Abrial preferred for functional perfumery where camphor character is desirable (soap, detergent, candle, air care); all cultivars share FEMA 2618 GRAS and the same EINECS CAS 91722-69-9

odor.description: Sharp, herbaceous, peppermint-camphoraceous with characteristic ketonic-cooling minty bite; fundamentally NON-cineole driven — 40-55% piperitone (vs the typical cineole-led eucalyptus profile), with α-phellandrene + p-cymene secondary notes; smells far more 'mint-eucalyptus' than 'medicinal-eucalyptus'

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — piperitone-rich chemotype; cineole low; commercial source for piperitone isolation → industrial menthone/menthol synthesis route; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; not a primary fine-fragrance feedstock
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for peppermint-eucalyptus / herbal-cooling accords; major industrial use is piperitone isolation (precursor to synthetic menthol); aromatherapy + cleaning + functional

odor.description: Sweet, fruity-floral, herbaceous with characteristic apple-pear-honey signature; softer + more luminous than Roman chamomile — dominated by 2-methylbutyl + isoamyl 2-methylpropanoate (isobutyrate) esters; deep blue color from chamazulene traces; distinctive honeyed-tea undertone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Eriocephalus punctulatus oil: no specific concentration restriction; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; FEMA n/a (not a flavour ingredient)
safety.usage: Typical 0.2-2% in fine fragrance for fruity-honey-chamomile / Cape-natural / niche-luxury accords; premium 4th-species chamomile favoured by South African natural houses + niche luxury perfumery (Mizensir, Maison Crivelli)

odor.description: Soft, dry, fresh-cypress with characteristic 'Japanese-onsen-spa' lemony-resinous undertone and subtle citrus-conifer depth; defining hinokitiol + sabinene + α-pinene + cadinene; iconic Japanese aromatherapy + Zen architecture wood; modern niche cornerstone (Comme des Garçons Series 5 Sherwood, Maison Margiela Across Sands)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Chamaecyparis obtusa oil: peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; cumulative cap with monoterpene hydrocarbons; sustainability certified (CITES considered)
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance for hinoki / Zen-Japanese / coniferous-niche accords

odor.description: Brighter, greener, more tea-like than grandiflorum with lighter indolic profile; methyl anthranilate + α-farnesene lift over sweet-honey floral base; Arabian / Indian jasmine character

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Jasminum sambac absolute: caps parallel to grandiflorum (Cat.4 ≈ 4%); allergen profile similar with higher linalool + methyl anthranilate and lower indole; EU 26 allergen labelling applies
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-2% in fine fragrance; preferred over grandiflorum for green-tea-tropical nuance; central to modern niche florals (e.g. Serge Lutens À la Nuit)

odor.description: Bright, lemony, fresh-camphoraceous with rich citral-lemon top and soft cineolic undertone; distinct from E. citriodora (citronellal-rich) by being CITRAL-rich (25-40% citral, ~20% limonene, ~10% cineole, ~10% geraniol) — closer to lemon verbena × eucalyptus than to citronella

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — caps cascade from citral content (Cat.4 ≈ 0.6% leave-on for sensitiser dose); EU 26 allergen disclosure (citral, limonene, geraniol); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for lemon-eucalyptus / citral-herbal accords; emerging Brazilian commercial supply; functional fragrance + aromatherapy + insect repellent (citral mosquito-repellent activity)

odor.description: Sharp, fresh, intensely-citrus with characteristic 'Thai-kaffir-leaf-lime' citronellal-rich green-citrus undertone; defining 60-80% citronellal + linalool + β-pinene; iconic Thai/SE Asian culinary citrus; modern niche-Asian fragrance material

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-5 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Citrus hystrix oil: cumulative cap with citrus oils; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; commercial supply via Thailand/Indonesia
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance for Thai-citrus / Asian-niche / fresh-green accords

odor.description: Delicate, soft, ethereal-floral-tea with characteristic 'silver-needle-trichome' silky-honeyed undertone; whitest most-pristine tea (only youngest unopened buds); subtler vs. green tea — more powdery-floral, less grassy; iconic Bvlgari Eau Parfumée Au Thé Blanc + Elizabeth Arden Pure White Linen + Estée Lauder White Linen-class signature; modern niche clean-accord cornerstone

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Camellia sinensis absolute (white): no specific concentration restriction; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; commercial supply rare (~$2000-4000/kg)
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance for white-tea / clean-pristine / Asian-luxury accords; pairs with linalool + ionones for soft-floral tea effects

odor.description: Soft, fresh, camphoraceous-cineolic with rounded α-terpineol-floral undertone; gentler and less sharp than E. globulus — 65-75% 1,8-cineole + ~10-15% α-terpineol drive a softer pharmacy-aromatic profile favoured for paediatric and inhalation aromatherapy applications

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — caps cascade from cineole content; no standalone oil entry; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen (limonene traces); softer respiratory profile than globulus — preferred chemotype for baby-care + inhalation
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-2% in fine fragrance; widely used in functional perfumery + aromatherapy (favoured over globulus for paediatric formulations); cold/cough rubs, candle, soap

odor.description: Sharp, crisp, lemon-citron with characteristic 'cedrat-fruit-zest' citron-pithy-bitter undertone; large yellow Citrus medica variant; Mediterranean classical material — French/Italian Riviera Eau de Cologne tradition

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — cedrat oil: phototoxic; cumulative cap; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for cedrat / classical-French-Italian / Mediterranean accords

odor.description: Intense, ultra-clean, sharp camphoraceous with near-pure 1,8-cineole character and minimal terpenic background; the highest-cineole commercial eucalyptus (88-92% 1,8-cineole) — pharmacopoeia (BP / USP / Ph.Eur.) grade benchmark for eucalyptus oil with the cleanest medicinal profile

performance.tenacity: Low to Medium — 2-4 hours on strip

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — pharma-grade cineole content (≥88%); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; concentrated inhalation respiratory irritant — avoid Cat.5D baby-product use; pharmacopoeia monograph compliant (BP/USP/Ph.Eur.)
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-2% in fine fragrance; primary commercial supply for pharma cineole + cough/cold preparations + mouthwash + camphor-medicinal accords; widely used in functional + pharma; Australian commercial industry standard

odor.description: Fresh, intensely green, crushed-grass with characteristic 'leaf alcohol' signature of freshly-cut foliage; iconic natural green — present in all green plants at 1-100 ppm; unique universal-green character

safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance; cornerstone of green accords — apple / violet leaf / tomato leaf / cucumber; overdose risks 'cannabis-like' off-note

odor.description: Warm, sweet, clove-spicy with soft herbaceous-anisic body and faint balsamic-phenolic undertone; rounder and less sharp than eugenol; defining note of Holy Basil (10-30%), Rose (trace), Bay, Citronella (2-5%); bee attractant used in fruit-fly lure traps (orchard pest management)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 restriction — IARC 2B possibly carcinogenic to humans; SCCS opinion flags genotoxic-carcinogen concern; Standard severely restricts leave-on use: Cat.1 ≤ 0.00002%, Cat.2 ≤ 0.000004%, Cat.3 ≤ 0.0002%, Cat.4 ≤ 0.002%, Cat.5A ≤ 0.0004%, Cat.5B ≤ 0.0002%, Cat.5D ≤ 0.00002% (effectively withdrawn from baby products), Cat.11A ≤ 0.00002%; EU Regulation 1334/2008 ML in flavouring (20 mg/kg alcoholic beverages, 2 mg/kg non-alcoholic); GHS H302 harmful if swallowed, H317 sensitiser, H335 respiratory irritation, H341 suspected mutagen, H350 may cause cancer (CLP Cat. 1B), H373 target-organ toxicity (repeated exposure)
safety.usage: Typical <0.001% in fine fragrance; trace-use only as isolated compound; more commonly encountered as a background component of natural oils (Holy Basil, Rose, Bay) where its level is controlled by oil fraction rather than isolation; fine-fragrance formulators should avoid adding as neat isolate given IFRA caps and health concerns; FEMA 2475 GRAS for low-ppm flavouring under EU 1334/2008 ML; not a routine perfumery tool — retained for completeness and understanding of Holy Basil / Tulsi / Rose composition

odor.description: Soft, sweet, floral-spicy with characteristic 'carnation-clove-warm' anisic-balsamic undertone; smoother + less harsh than isoeugenol; major synthetic carnation accord molecule; widely used in floral-spice fragrance

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — methyl isoeugenol: cumulative cap with isoeugenol + methyl eugenol class (genotoxicity concerns from methylenedioxy/methoxy phenylpropanoid); not on EU 26 allergen list directly but flagged for safrole-class concern; FEMA 2748 GRAS at trace; pregnancy contra-indicated
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for carnation / spicy-floral / clove-pink accords

odor.description: Mild, sweet, clove-floral with carnation-like warmth; much softer and rounder than eugenol; fixative character in oriental-spicy compositions

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction on eugenyl acetate itself; hydrolyzes slowly on skin to eugenol (EU 26 allergen disclosure required via the eugenol moiety); FEMA 2471 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-3% in fine fragrance for soft spicy-carnation accords; natural component of clove bud oil (2-5%); preferred over raw eugenol where softer character wanted; not a standalone skin sensitiser but disclosure required due to hydrolysis

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction on piperine; non-volatile alkaloid not typically used in fine fragrance (<0.1% solubility limit); FEMA 2909 GRAS for flavour; oral LD50 rat 514 mg/kg

odor.description: Bright, fresh, citral-rich floral with characteristic 'magnolia-lemon-blossom' green-citrusy floral undertone; defining linalool + citral + methyl eugenol profile; lighter + more lemon-fresh than rose/jasmine; modern niche material (Estée Lauder Magnolia Macarena, Chloé Rose Magnolia)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Magnolia spp. absolute: cumulative cap with floral absolutes; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L; EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + citral)
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for magnolia / luxury-floral / citral-floral accords

odor.description: Deep, dark, animalic-leathery-woody with characteristic 'barnyard-stable' fecal-resinous-balsamic profile and powerful smoky-medicinal undertone; defining Indian/Assam oud chemotype — heaviest, most fermented, animalic of all ouds; agarofuran + jinkohol + agarospirol + 2-(2-phenylethyl)chromone driven; ultra-premium luxury (~$30000-100000/kg pure)

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Aquilaria malaccensis oil: no specific concentration restriction; CITES Appendix II (regulated trade — sustainability sourcing required); EU 26 allergen labelling (linalool + traces); peroxide ≤20 mmol/L
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-2% in fine fragrance for oud / Middle Eastern luxury / smoky-leather accords; Hindi grade prized for animalic depth (vs. cleaner Cambodian)

odor.description: Warm, sweet, intensely spicy-clove with phenolic-medicinal bite and soft balsamic-caryophyllic undertone; defining compound of clove bud (70-85%), clove leaf (70-90%), and Holy Basil (30-60%); significant in cinnamon leaf, bay, allspice; the archetypal 'clove' note in perfumery and dental/antiseptic applications

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 restriction — skin sensitiser (Schedule B); caps: Cat.1 = 0.035%, Cat.2 = 0.1%, Cat.3 = 0.4%, Cat.4 = 1.2%, Cat.5A = 0.26%, Cat.5B = 0.18%, Cat.5C = 0.36%, Cat.5D = 0.055%, Cat.9 = 0.31%, Cat.10A = 1.2%, Cat.10B = 1.2%, Cat.11A = 0.042%, Cat.11B = 0.085%, Cat.12 = not restricted; EU 26 mandatory allergen labelling (>10 ppm leave-on / >100 ppm rinse-off); FEMA 2467 GRAS; GHS H302 harmful, H315 skin irritation, H317 skin sensitiser, H319 eye irritation
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance; widely used in spicy / oriental / carnation / gourmand / amber compositions; dental and oral-care applications (analgesic zinc-eugenol cement, eugenol-based mouthwash); food flavouring under FEMA 2467 (baked goods, chewing gum, confectionery, clove-spiced meats); industrial (perfumery, pharmaceutical, bee-hive repellent)

odor.description: Sweet, floral-spicy, carnation-like with clove-phenolic warmth; softer and more floral than eugenol; classical 'carnation' perfumery material

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — Cat.1 = 0.01%, Cat.2 = 0.003%, Cat.3 = 0.02%, Cat.4 = 0.08%, Cat.5A = 0.02%, Cat.5B = 0.01%, Cat.5C = 0.02%, Cat.5D = 0.003%, Cat.9 = 0.14%, Cat.10A-B = 0.14%, Cat.11A-B = 0.07%, Cat.12 Not Limited; strong skin sensitiser H317 (stricter caps than eugenol); EU 26 allergen; FEMA 2468 GRAS
safety.usage: Typical 0.01-0.05% in fine fragrance for carnation / oriental / spicy-floral accords; skin sensitiser disclosure required; oxidises to vanillin + isovanillin on storage (use amber + antioxidant)

odor.description: Soft, mild, honeyed-floral with characteristic 'European-linden' green-tea-honey balsamic undertone; synthetic linden accord (natural Tilia extraction limited); built from farnesol + nerolidol + linalool + benzaldehyde + hexenol

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — linden accord: cumulative cap with constituents
safety.usage: Typical 0.5-2% in fine fragrance for linden / honey-tea-floral / French-classical accords

odor.description: Rich, warm, hay-like, coumarinic and balsamic; deeper and sweeter than the oil with herbaceous-tobacco undertone

safety.usage: Typical 0.1-2% in fine fragrance; restricted in leave-on cosmetics due to coumarin content; used in functional perfumery (candle, soap, fabric)

odor.description: Fresh, sharp, peppery-citrus with strong terpene-woody character; more assertive than β-phellandrene with distinct dill/eucalyptus lift

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no specific concentration restriction; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (conjugated cyclohexadiene — highly prone to oxidation, hydroperoxide formation, and polymerization; sensitiser when oxidised); FEMA 2856 GRAS; BHT / tocopherol stabilisation strongly recommended
safety.usage: Typical 0.05-0.5% in fine fragrance for fresh-peppery accords; natural component of fennel (1-8%), dill seed (20-40%), eucalyptus (1-5%), black pepper, angelica; oxidised α-phellandrene is a known skin sensitiser — always use fresh stock with BHT

odor.description: Light, fresh, citrus-terpenic with slight bitter-herbaceous body and soft woody tail; biosynthetic precursor to thymol/carvacrol/p-cymene

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone concentration restriction; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (monoterpene autoxidation); FEMA 3559 GRAS as flavouring; GHS H226 flammable, H304 aspiration hazard, H411 aquatic toxicity — store cool/dark to limit hydroperoxide formation
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance; used in citrus-herbal accords and as diluent; aspiration hazard flagged; food-flavour compounding under FEMA 3559

odor.description: Fresh, citrusy-piney-herbaceous with characteristic 'lemon-pine' terpene profile; less harsh than γ-terpinene; precursor in thymol biosynthesis pathway (α-terpinene → γ-terpinene → p-cymene → thymol/carvacrol); widespread in tea tree, marjoram, cardamom

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — terpene hydrocarbons: peroxide ≤20 mmol/L mandatory (rapid air-oxidation to allergenic peroxides); FEMA 3558 GRAS; cumulative cap with other monoterpene hydrocarbons; aged α-terpinene = dermatitic
safety.usage: Typical trace 0.01-0.5% in fine fragrance — main industrial use as biosynthesis intermediate / chemotaxonomic marker; rarely added directly due to oxidation

odor.description: Fresh, light, terpenic-citrusy with soft woody-herbal undertone and faint gasoline-like tang; biosynthetic precursor to thymol/carvacrol

safety.ifra: IFRA 51 — no standalone concentration restriction for p-cymene; peroxide ≤20 mmol/L (autoxidation of the aromatic hydrocarbon); FEMA 2356 GRAS as flavouring; GHS H226 flammable, H304 aspiration hazard, H336 drowsiness/dizziness, H411 aquatic toxicity
safety.usage: Typical 0.1-1% in fine fragrance; used as diluent/top-note and in citrus-herbal accords; aspiration hazard flagged for handling; food-flavour compounding under FEMA 2356
